{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019VDQeznT9T1QZgvBL9wdfe","type":"tool_result","content":"{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2: Copyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n3: \\n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n7: \\n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n9: \\n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: package imagelocality\\n18: \\n19: import (\\n20: \\t\\\"context\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n23: \\n24: \\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n25: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n26: \\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/names\\\"\\n28: )\\n29: \\n30: // The two thresholds are used as bounds for the image score range. They correspond to a reasonable size range for\\n31: // container images compressed and stored in registries; 90%ile of images on dockerhub drops into this range.\\n32: const (\\n33: \\tmb                    int64 = 1024 * 1024\\n34: \\tminThreshold          int64 = 23 * mb\\n35: \\tmaxContainerThreshold int64 = 1000 * mb\\n36: )\\n37: \\n38: // ImageLocality is a score plugin that favors nodes that already have requested pod container's images.\\n39: type ImageLocality struct {\\n40: \\thandle framework.Handle\\n41: }\\n42: \\n43: var _ framework.ScorePlugin = &ImageLocality{}\\n44: \\n45: // Name is the name of the plugin used in the plugin registry and configurations.\\n46: const Name = names.ImageLocality\\n47: \\n48: // Name returns name of the plugin. It is used in logs, etc.\\n49: func (pl *ImageLocality) Name() string {\\n50: \\treturn Name\\n51: }\\n52: \\n53: // Score invoked at the score extension point.\\n54: func (pl *ImageLocality) Score(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *framework.Status) {\\n55: \\tnodeInfo, err := pl.handle.SnapshotSharedLister().NodeInfos().Get(nodeName)\\n56: \\tif err != nil {\\n57: \\t\\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\\\"getting node %q from Snapshot: %w\\\", nodeName, err))\\n58: \\t}\\n59: \\n60: \\tnodeInfos, err := pl.handle.SnapshotSharedLister().NodeInfos().List()\\n61: \\tif err != nil {\\n62: \\t\\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(err)\\n63: \\t}\\n64: \\ttotalNumNodes := len(nodeInfos)\\n65: \\n66: \\timageScores := sumImageScores(nodeInfo, pod, totalNumNodes)\\n67: \\tscore := calculatePriority(imageScores, len(pod.Spec.InitContainers)+len(pod.Spec.Containers))\\n68: \\n69: \\treturn score, nil\\n70: }\\n71: \\n72: // ScoreExtensions of the Score plugin.\\n73: func (pl *ImageLocality) ScoreExtensions() framework.ScoreExtensions {\\n74: \\treturn nil\\n75: }\\n76: \\n77: // New initializes a new plugin and returns it.\\n78: func New(_ context.Context, _ runtime.Object, h framework.Handle) (framework.Plugin, error) {\\n79: \\treturn &ImageLocality{handle: h}, nil\\n80: }\\n81: \\n82: // calculatePriority returns the priority of a node. Given the sumScores of requested images on the node, the node's\\n83: // priority is obtained by scaling the maximum priority value with a ratio proportional to the sumScores.\\n84: func calculatePriority(sumScores int64, numContainers int) int64 {\\n85: \\tmaxThreshold := maxContainerThreshold * int64(numContainers)\\n86: \\tif sumScores < minThreshold {\\n87: \\t\\tsumScores = minThreshold\\n88: \\t} else if sumScores > maxThreshold {\\n89: \\t\\tsumScores = maxThreshold\\n90: \\t}\\n91: \\n92: \\treturn framework.MaxNodeScore * (sumScores - minThreshold) / (maxThreshold - minThreshold)\\n93: }\\n94: \\n95: // sumImageScores returns the sum of image scores of all the containers that are already on the node.\\n96: // Each image receives a raw score of its size, scaled by scaledImageScore. The raw scores are later used to calculate\\n97: // the final score.\\n98: func sumImageScores(nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo, pod *v1.Pod, totalNumNodes int) int64 {\\n99: \\tvar sum int64\\n100: \\tfor _, container := range pod.Spec.InitContainers {\\n101: \\t\\tif state, ok := nodeInfo.ImageStates[normalizedImageName(container.Image)]; ok {\\n102: \\t\\t\\tsum += scaledImageScore(state, totalNumNodes)\\n103: \\t\\t}\\n104: \\t}\\n105: \\tfor _, container := range pod.Spec.Containers {\\n106: \\t\\tif state, ok := nodeInfo.ImageStates[normalizedImageName(container.Image)]; ok {\\n107: \\t\\t\\tsum += scaledImageScore(state, totalNumNodes)\\n108: \\t\\t}\\n109: \\t}\\n110: \\treturn sum\\n111: }\\n112: \\n113: // scaledImageScore returns an adaptively scaled score for the given state of an image.\\n114: // The size of the image is used as the base score, scaled by a factor which considers how much nodes the image has \\\"spread\\\" to.\\n115: // This heuristic aims to mitigate the undesirable \\\"node heating problem\\\", i.e., pods get assigned to the same or\\n116: // a few nodes due to image locality.\\n117: func scaledImageScore(imageState *framework.ImageStateSummary, totalNumNodes int) int64 {\\n118: \\tspread := float64(imageState.NumNodes) / float64(totalNumNodes)\\n119: \\treturn int64(float64(imageState.Size) * spread)\\n120: }\\n121: \\n122: // normalizedImageName returns the CRI compliant name for a given image.\\n123: // TODO: cover the corner cases of missed matches, e.g,\\n124: // 1. Using Docker as runtime and docker.io/library/test:tag in pod spec, but only test:tag will present in node status\\n125: // 2. \\treturn s\\n108: }\\n109: \\n110: // preScoreState computed at PreScore and used at Score.\\n111: type preScoreState struct {\\n112: \\t// podRequests have the same order as the resources defined in Because init-containers run sequentially, we collect\\n182: // the max in each dimension iteratively. In contrast, we sum the resource vectors for\\n183: // regular containers since they run simultaneously.\\n184: //\\n185: // # The resources defined for Overhead should be added to the calculated Resource request sum\\n186: //\\n187: // Example:\\n188: //\\n189: // Pod:\\n190: //\\n191: //\\tInitContainers\\n192: //\\t  IC1:\\n193: //\\t    CPU: 2\\n194: //\\t    Memory: 1G\\n195: //\\t  IC2:\\n196: //\\t    CPU: 2\\n197: //\\t    Memory: 3G\\n198: //\\tContainers\\n199: //\\t  C1:\\n200: //\\t    CPU: 2\\n201: //\\t    Memory: 1G\\n202: //\\t  C2:\\n203: //\\t    CPU: 1\\n204: //\\t    Memory: 1G\\n205: //\\n206: // Result: CPU: 3, Memory: 3G\\n207: func computePodResourceRequest(pod *v1.Pod) *preFilterState {\\n208: \\t// pod hasn't scheduled yet so we don't need to worry about InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled\\n209: \\treqs := resource.PodRequests(pod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{})\\n210: \\tresult := &preFilterState{}\\n211: \\tresult.SetMaxResource(reqs)\\n212: \\treturn result\\n213: }\\n214: \\n215: // PreFilter invoked at the prefilter extension point.\\n216: func (f *Fit) PreFilter(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (*framework.PreFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\\n217: \\tif !f.enableSidecarContainers && hasRestartableInitContainer(pod) {\\n218: \\t\\t// Scheduler will calculate resources usage for a Pod containing\\n219: \\t\\t// restartable init containers that will be equal or more than kubelet will\\n220: \\t\\t// require to run the Pod. So there will be no overbooking. However, to\\n221: \\t\\t// avoid the inconsistency in resource calculation between the scheduler\\n222: \\t\\t// and the older (before v1.28) kubelet, make the Pod unschedulable.\\n223: \\t\\treturn nil, framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, \\\"Pod has a restartable init container and the SidecarContainers feature is disabled\\\")\\n224: \\t}\\n225: \\tcycleState.Write(preFilterStateKey, computePodResourceRequest(pod))\\n226: \\treturn nil, nil\\n227: }\\n228: \\n229: // PreFilterExtensions returns prefilter extensions, pod add and remove.\\n230: func (f *Fit) PreFilterExtensions() framework.PreFilterExtensions {\\n231: \\treturn nil\\n232: }\\n233: \\n234: func getPreFilterState(cycleState *framework.CycleState) (*preFilterState, error) {\\n235: \\tc, err := cycleState.Read(preFilterStateKey)\\n236: \\tif err != nil {\\n237: \\t\\t// preFilterState doesn't exist, likely PreFilter wasn't invoked.\\n238: \\t\\treturn nil, updated. It checks whether\\n264: // that change made a previously unschedulable pod schedulable.\\n265: func (f *Fit) isSchedulableAfterPodChange(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, oldObj, newObj interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\\n266: \\toriginalPod, modifiedPod, err := schedutil.As[*v1.Pod](oldObj, newObj)\\n267: \\tif err != nil {\\n268: \\t\\treturn framework.Queue, err\\n269: \\t}\\n270: \\n271: \\tif modifiedPod == nil {\\n272: \\t\\tif originalPod.Spec.NodeName == \\\"\\\" {\\n273: \\t\\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"the deleted pod was unscheduled and it wouldn't make the unscheduled pod schedulable\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"deletedPod\\\", klog.KObj(originalPod))\\n274: \\t\\t\\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\\n275: \\t\\t}\\n276: \\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"another scheduled pod was deleted, and it may make the unscheduled pod schedulable\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"deletedPod\\\", klog.KObj(originalPod))\\n277: \\t\\treturn framework.Queue, nil\\n278: \\t}\\n279: \\n280: \\tif !f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling {\\n281: \\t\\t// If InPlacePodVerticalScaling (KEP 1287) is disabled, it cannot free up resources.\\n282: \\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"another pod was modified, but InPlacePodVerticalScaling is disabled, so it doesn't make the unscheduled pod schedulable\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"modifiedPod\\\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod))\\n283: \\t\\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\\n284: \\t}\\n285: \\n286: \\t// Modifications may or may not be relevant. We only care about modifications that\\n287: \\t// change the other pod's resource request and the resource is also requested by the\\n288: \\t// pod we are trying to schedule.\\n289: \\tif !f.isResourceScaleDown(pod, originalPod, modifiedPod) {\\n290: \\t\\tif loggerV := logger.V(10); loggerV.Enabled() {\\n291: \\t\\t\\t// Log more information.\\n292: \\t\\t\\tloggerV.Info(\\\"another Pod got modified, but the modification isn't related to the resource request\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"modifiedPod\\\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod), \\\"diff\\\", cmp.Diff(originalPod, }\\n302: \\n303: // isResourceScaleDown checks whether the resource request of the modified pod is less than the original pod\\n304: // for the resources requested by the pod we are trying to schedule.\\n305: func (f *Fit) isResourceScaleDown(targetPod, originalOtherPod, modifiedOtherPod *v1.Pod) bool {\\n306: \\tif modifiedOtherPod.Spec.NodeName == \\\"\\\" {\\n307: \\t\\t// no resource is freed up whatever the pod is modified.\\n308: \\t\\treturn false\\n309: \\t}\\n310: \\n311: \\t// the other pod was scheduled, so modification or deletion may free up some resources.\\n312: \\toriginalMaxResourceReq, modifiedMaxResourceReq := &framework.Resource{}, &framework.Resource{}\\n313: \\toriginalMaxResourceReq.SetMaxResource(resource.PodRequests(originalOtherPod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled: f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling}))\\n314: \\tmodifiedMaxResourceReq.SetMaxResource(resource.PodRequests(modifiedOtherPod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled: f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling}))\\n315: \\n316: \\t// check whether the resource request of the modified pod is less than the original pod.\\n317: \\tpodRequests := resource.PodRequests(targetPod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled: f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling})\\n318: \\tfor rName, rValue := \\t\\tswitch rName {\\n324: \\t\\tcase v1.ResourceCPU:\\n325: \\t\\t\\tif originalMaxResourceReq.MilliCPU > modifiedMaxResourceReq.MilliCPU {\\n326: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n327: \\t\\t\\t}\\n328: \\t\\tdefault:\\n337: \\t\\t\\tif schedutil.IsScalarResourceName(rName) && originalMaxResourceReq.ScalarResources[rName] > modifiedMaxResourceReq.ScalarResources[rName] {\\n338: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n339: \\t\\t\\t}\\n340: \\t\\t}\\n341: \\t}\\n342: \\treturn false\\n343: }\\n344: \\n345: // isSchedulableAfterNodeChange is invoked whenever a node added or changed. \\n374: // Filter invoked at the filter extension point.\\n375: // Checks if a node has sufficient resources, such as cpu, memory, gpu, opaque int resources etc to run a pod.\\n376: // It returns a list of insufficient resources, if empty, then the node has all the resources requested by the pod.\\n377: func (f *Fit) Filter(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, It is used in logs, etc.\\n146: func (f *Fit) Name() string {\\n147: \\treturn Name\\n148: }\\n149: \\n150: // NewFit initializes a new plugin and returns it.\\n151: func NewFit(_ context.Context, plArgs runtime.Object, h framework.Handle, fts feature.Features) (framework.Plugin, error) {\\n152: \\targs, ok := plArgs.(*config.NodeResourcesFitArgs)\\n153: \\tif !ok {\\n154: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"want args to be of type NodeResourcesFitArgs, got %T\\\", plArgs)\\n155: \\t}\\n156: \\tif err := validation.ValidateNodeResourcesFitArgs(nil, args); err != nil {\\n157: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n158: \\t}\\n159: \\n160: \\tif args.ScoringStrategy == nil {\\n161: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"scoring strategy not specified\\\")\\n162: \\t}\\n163: \\n164: \\tstrategy := args.ScoringStrategy.Type\\n165: \\tscorePlugin, exists := nodeResourceStrategyTypeMap[strategy]\\n166: \\tif !exists {\\n167: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"scoring strategy %s is not supported\\\", strategy)\\n168: \\t}\\n169: \\n170: \\treturn &Fit{\\n171: \\t\\tignoredResources:                sets.New(args.IgnoredResources...),\\n172: \\t\\tignoredResourceGroups:           sets.New(args.IgnoredResourceGroups...),\\n173: \\t\\tenableInPlacePodVerticalScaling: fts.EnableInPlacePodVerticalScaling,\\n174: \\t\\tenableSidecarContainers:         fts.EnableSidecarContainers,\\n175: \\t\\thandle:                          h,\\n176: \\t\\tresourceAllocationScorer:        *scorePlugin(args),\\n177: \\t}, nil\\n178: }\\n179: \\n180: // computePodResourceRequest returns a framework.Resource that covers the largest\\n181: // width in each resource dimension. Because init-containers run sequentially, we collect\\n182: // the max in each dimension iteratively. In contrast, we sum the resource vectors for\\n183: // regular containers since they run simultaneously.\\n184: //\\n185: // # The resources defined for Overhead should be added to the calculated Resource request sum\\n186: //\\n187: // Example:\\n188: //\\n189: // Pod:\\n190: //\\n191: //\\tInitContainers\\n192: //\\t  IC1:\\n193: //\\t    CPU: 2\\n194: //\\t    Memory: 1G\\n195: //\\t  IC2:\\n196: //\\t    CPU: 2\\n197: //\\t    Memory: 3G\\n198: //\\tContainers\\n199: //\\t  C1:\\n200: //\\t    CPU: 2\\n201: //\\t    Memory: 1G\\n202: //\\t  C2:\\n203: //\\t    CPU: 1\\n204: //\\t    Memory: 1G\\n205: //\\n206: // Result: CPU: 3, Memory: 3G\\n207: func computePodResourceRequest(pod *v1.Pod) *preFilterState {\\n208: \\t// pod hasn't scheduled yet so we don't need to worry about InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled\\n209: \\treqs := resource.PodRequests(pod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{})\\n210: \\tresult := &preFilterState{}\\n211: \\tresult.SetMaxResource(reqs)\\n212: \\treturn result\\n213: }\\n214: \\n215: // PreFilter invoked at the prefilter extension point.\\n216: func (f *Fit) PreFilter(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (*framework.PreFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\\n217: \\tif !f.enableSidecarContainers && hasRestartableInitContainer(pod) {\\n218: \\t\\t// Scheduler will calculate resources usage for a Pod containing\\n219: \\t\\t// restartable init containers that will be equal or more than kubelet will\\n220: \\t\\t// require to run the Pod. So there will be no overbooking. However, to\\n221: \\t\\t// avoid the inconsistency in resource calculation between the scheduler\\n222: \\t\\t// and the older (before v1.28) kubelet, make the Pod unschedulable.\\n223: \\t\\treturn nil, framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, \\\"Pod has a restartable init container and the SidecarContainers feature is disabled\\\")\\n224: \\t}\\n225: \\tcycleState.Write(preFilterStateKey, computePodResourceRequest(pod))\\n226: \\treturn nil, nil\\n227: }\\n228: \\n229: // PreFilterExtensions returns prefilter extensions, pod add and remove.\\n230: func (f *Fit) PreFilterExtensions() framework.PreFilterExtensions {\\n231: \\treturn nil\\n232: }\\n233: \\n234: func getPreFilterState(cycleState *framework.CycleState) (*preFilterState, error) {\\n235: \\tc, err := cycleState.Read(preFilterStateKey)\\n236: \\tif err != nil {\\n237: \\t\\t// preFilterState doesn't exist, likely PreFilter wasn't invoked.\\n238: \\t\\treturn nil, Pod\\n249: // failed by this plugin schedulable.\\n250: func (f *Fit) EventsToRegister() []framework.ClusterEventWithHint {\\n251: \\tpodActionType := framework.Delete\\n252: \\tif f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling {\\n253: \\t\\t// If InPlacePodVerticalScaling (KEP 1287) is enabled, then PodUpdate event should be registered\\n254: \\t\\t// for this plugin since a Pod update may framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Pod, ActionType: podActionType}, QueueingHintFn: f.isSchedulableAfterPodChange},\\n259: \\t\\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Node, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}, QueueingHintFn: f.isSchedulableAfterNodeChange},\\n260: \\t}\\n261: }\\n262: \\n263: // isSchedulableAfterPodChange is invoked whenever a pod deleted or updated. It checks whether\\n264: // that change made a previously unschedulable pod schedulable.\\n265: func (f *Fit) isSchedulableAfterPodChange(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, oldObj, newObj interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\\n266: \\toriginalPod, modifiedPod, err := schedutil.As[*v1.Pod](oldObj, newObj)\\n267: \\tif err != nil {\\n268: \\t\\treturn framework.Queue, err\\n269: \\t}\\n270: \\n271: \\tif modifiedPod == nil {\\n272: \\t\\tif originalPod.Spec.NodeName == \\\"\\\" {\\n273: \\t\\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"the deleted pod was unscheduled and it wouldn't make the unscheduled pod schedulable\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"deletedPod\\\", klog.KObj(originalPod))\\n274: \\t\\t\\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\\n275: \\t\\t}\\n276: \\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"another scheduled pod was deleted, and it may make the unscheduled pod schedulable\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"deletedPod\\\", klog.KObj(originalPod))\\n277: \\t\\treturn framework.Queue, nil\\n278: \\t}\\n279: \\n280: \\tif !f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling {\\n281: \\t\\t// If InPlacePodVerticalScaling (KEP 1287) is disabled, it cannot free up resources.\\n282: \\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"another pod was modified, but InPlacePodVerticalScaling is disabled, so it doesn't make the unscheduled pod schedulable\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"modifiedPod\\\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod))\\n283: \\t\\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\\n284: \\t}\\n285: \\n286: \\t// Modifications may or may not be relevant. We only care about modifications that\\n287: \\t// change the other pod's resource request and the resource is also requested by the\\n288: \\t// pod we are trying to schedule.\\n289: \\tif !f.isResourceScaleDown(pod, originalPod, modifiedPod) {\\n290: \\t\\tif loggerV := logger.V(10); loggerV.Enabled() {\\n291: \\t\\t\\t// Log more information.\\n292: \\t\\t\\tloggerV.Info(\\\"another Pod got modified, but the modification isn't related to the resource request\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"modifiedPod\\\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod), \\\"diff\\\", cmp.Diff(originalPod, modifiedPod))\\n293: \\t\\t} else {\\n294: \\t\\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"another Pod got modified, but the modification isn't related to the resource request\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), }\\n302: \\n303: // isResourceScaleDown checks whether the resource request of the modified pod is less than the original pod\\n304: // for the resources requested by the pod we are trying to schedule.\\n305: func (f *Fit) isResourceScaleDown(targetPod, originalOtherPod, modifiedOtherPod *v1.Pod) bool {\\n306: \\tif modifiedOtherPod.Spec.NodeName == \\\"\\\" {\\n307: \\t\\t// no resource is freed up whatever the pod is modified.\\n308: \\t\\treturn false\\n309: \\t}\\n310: \\n311: \\t// the other pod was scheduled, so modification or deletion may free up some resources.\\n312: \\toriginalMaxResourceReq, modifiedMaxResourceReq := &framework.Resource{}, &framework.Resource{}\\n313: \\toriginalMaxResourceReq.SetMaxResource(resource.PodRequests(originalOtherPod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled: f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling}))\\n314: \\tmodifiedMaxResourceReq.SetMaxResource(resource.PodRequests(modifiedOtherPod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled: f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling}))\\n315: \\n316: \\t// check whether the resource request of the modified pod is less than the original pod.\\n317: \\tpodRequests := resource.PodRequests(targetPod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled: f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling})\\n318: \\tfor rName, rValue := \\t\\tswitch rName {\\n324: \\t\\tcase v1.ResourceCPU:\\n325: \\t\\t\\tif originalMaxResourceReq.MilliCPU > modifiedMaxResourceReq.MilliCPU {\\n326: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n327: \\t\\t\\t}\\n328: \\t\\tcase v1.ResourceMemory:\\n329: \\t\\t\\tif originalMaxResourceReq.Memory > modifiedMaxResourceReq.Memory {\\n330: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n331: \\t\\t\\t}\\n332: \\t\\tcase v1.ResourceEphemeralStorage:\\n333: \\t\\t\\tif originalMaxResourceReq.EphemeralStorage > modifiedMaxResourceReq.EphemeralStorage {\\n334: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n335: \\t\\t\\t}\\n336: \\t\\tdefault:\\n337: \\t\\t\\tif schedutil.IsScalarResourceName(rName) && originalMaxResourceReq.ScalarResources[rName] > modifiedMaxResourceReq.ScalarResources[rName] {\\n338: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n339: \\t\\t\\t}\\n340: \\t\\t}\\n341: \\t}\\n342: \\treturn false\\n343: }\\n344: \\n345: // isSchedulableAfterNodeChange is invoked whenever a node added or changed. It checks whether\\n346: // that change made a previously unschedulable pod schedulable.\\n347: func (f *Fit) isSchedulableAfterNodeChange(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, oldObj, newObj interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\\n348: \\t_, modifiedNode, err := schedutil.As[*v1.Node](oldObj, newObj)\\n349: \\tif err != nil {\\n350: \\t\\treturn framework.Queue, err\\n351: \\t}\\n352: \\t// TODO: also check if the original node meets the pod's resource requestments once preCheck is completely removed.\\n353: \\t// See: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/110175\\n354: \\tif isFit(pod, modifiedNode) {\\n355: \\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"node was updated, and may fit with the pod's resource requestments\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"node\\\", klog.KObj(modifiedNode))\\n356: \\t\\treturn framework.Queue, nil\\n357: \\t}\\n358: \\n359: \\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"node was created or updated, but it doesn't have enough resource(s) to accommodate this pod\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"node\\\", klog.KObj(modifiedNode))\\n360: \\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\\n361: }\\n362: \\n363: // isFit checks if the pod fits the node. \\n374: // Filter invoked at the filter extension point.\\n375: // Checks if a node has sufficient resources, such as cpu, memory, gpu, opaque int resources etc to run a pod.\\n376: // It returns a list of insufficient resources, if empty, then the node has all the resources requested by the pod.\\n377: func (f *Fit) Filter(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, args.ScoringStrategy.Resources\n77: \t\treturn &resourceAllocationScorer{\n78: \t\t\tName:      string(config.RequestedToCapacityRatio),\n79: \t\t\tscorer:    requestedToCapacityRatioScorer(resources, args.ScoringStrategy.RequestedToCapacityRatio.Shape),\n80: \t\t\tresources: resources,\n81: \t\t}\n82: \t},\n83: }\n84: \n85: // Fit is a plugin that checks if a node has sufficient resources.\n86: It is used in logs, etc.\n146: func (f *Fit) Name() string {\n147: \treturn Name\n148: }\n149: \n150: // NewFit initializes a new plugin and returns it.\n151: func NewFit(_ context.Context, plArgs runtime.Object, h framework.Handle, fts feature.Features) (framework.Plugin, error) {\n152: \targs, ok := plArgs.(*config.NodeResourcesFitArgs)\n153: \tif !ok {\n154: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"want args to be of type NodeResourcesFitArgs, got %T\", plArgs)\n155: \t}\n156: \tif err := validation.ValidateNodeResourcesFitArgs(nil, args); err != nil {\n157: \t\treturn nil, err\n158: \t}\n159: \n160: \tif args.ScoringStrategy == nil {\n161: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"scoring strategy not specified\")\n162: \t}\n163: \n164: \tstrategy := args.ScoringStrategy.Type\n165: \tscorePlugin, exists := nodeResourceStrategyTypeMap[strategy]\n166: \tif !exists {\n167: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"scoring strategy %s is not supported\", strategy)\n168: \t}\n169: \n170: \treturn &Fit{\n171: \t\tignoredResources:                sets.New(args.IgnoredResources...),\n172: \t\tignoredResourceGroups:           sets.New(args.IgnoredResourceGroups...),\n173: \t\tenableInPlacePodVerticalScaling: fts.EnableInPlacePodVerticalScaling,\n174: \t\tenableSidecarContainers:         fts.EnableSidecarContainers,\n175: \t\thandle:                          h,\n176: \t\tresourceAllocationScorer:        *scorePlugin(args),\n177: \t}, nil\n178: }\n179: \n180: // computePodResourceRequest returns a framework.Resource that covers the largest\n181: // width in each resource dimension. Because init-containers run sequentially, we collect\n182: // the max in each dimension iteratively. In contrast, we sum the resource vectors for\n183: // regular containers since they run simultaneously.\n184: //\n185: // # The resources defined for Overhead should be added to the calculated Resource request sum\n186: //\n187: // Example:\n188: //\n189: // Pod:\n190: //\n191: //\tInitContainers\n192: //\t  IC1:\n193: //\t    CPU: 2\n194: //\t    Memory: 1G\n195: //\t  IC2:\n196: //\t    CPU: 2\n197: //\t    Memory: 3G\n198: //\tContainers\n199: //\t  C1:\n200: //\t    CPU: 2\n201: //\t    Memory: 1G\n202: //\t  C2:\n203: //\t    CPU: 1\n204: //\t    Memory: 1G\n205: //\n206: // Result: CPU: 3, Memory: 3G\n207: func computePodResourceRequest(pod *v1.Pod) *preFilterState {\n208: \t// pod hasn't scheduled yet so we don't need to worry about InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled\n209: \treqs := resource.PodRequests(pod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{})\n210: \tresult := &preFilterState{}\n211: \tresult.SetMaxResource(reqs)\n212: \treturn result\n213: }\n214: \n215: // PreFilter invoked at the prefilter extension point.\n216: func (f *Fit) PreFilter(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (*framework.PreFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\n217: \tif !f.enableSidecarContainers && hasRestartableInitContainer(pod) {\n218: \t\t// Scheduler will calculate resources usage for a Pod containing\n219: \t\t// restartable init containers that will be equal or more than kubelet will\n220: \t\t// require to run the Pod. So there will be no overbooking. However, to\n221: \t\t// avoid the inconsistency in resource calculation between the scheduler\n222: \t\t// and the older (before v1.28) kubelet, make the Pod unschedulable.\n223: \t\treturn nil, framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, \"Pod has a restartable init container and the SidecarContainers feature is disabled\")\n224: \t}\n225: \tcycleState.Write(preFilterStateKey, computePodResourceRequest(pod))\n226: \treturn nil, nil\n227: }\n228: \n229: // PreFilterExtensions returns prefilter extensions, pod add and remove.\n230: func (f *Fit) PreFilterExtensions() framework.PreFilterExtensions {\n231: \treturn nil\n232: }\n233: \n234: func getPreFilterState(cycleState *framework.CycleState) (*preFilterState, error) {\n235: \tc, error\", c)\n244: \t}\n245: \treturn s, nil\n246: }\n247: \n248: // EventsToRegister returns the possible events that may make a Pod\n249: // failed by this plugin schedulable.\n250: func (f *Fit) EventsToRegister() []framework.ClusterEventWithHint {\n251: \tpodActionType := framework.Delete\n252: \tif f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling {\n253: \t\t// If InPlacePodVerticalScaling (KEP 1287) is enabled, then PodUpdate event should be registered\n254: \t\t// for this plugin since a Pod update may free up resources that make other Pods schedulable.\n255: \t\tpodActionType |= framework.Update\n256: \t}\n257: \treturn []framework.ClusterEventWithHint{\n258: \t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Pod, ActionType: podActionType}, QueueingHintFn: f.isSchedulableAfterPodChange},\n259: \t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Node, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}, QueueingHintFn: f.isSchedulableAfterNodeChange},\n260: \t}\n261: }\n262: \n263: // isSchedulableAfterPodChange is invoked whenever a pod deleted or updated. It checks whether\n264: // that change made a previously unschedulable pod schedulable.\n265: func (f *Fit) isSchedulableAfterPodChange(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, oldObj, newObj interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\n266: \toriginalPod, modifiedPod, err := schedutil.As[*v1.Pod](oldObj, newObj)\n267: \tif err != nil {\n268: \t\treturn framework.Queue, err\n269: \t}\n270: \n271: \tif modifiedPod == nil {\n272: \t\tif originalPod.Spec.NodeName == \"\" {\n273: \t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"the deleted pod was unscheduled and it wouldn't make the unscheduled pod schedulable\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"deletedPod\", klog.KObj(originalPod))\n274: \t\t\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\n275: \t\t}\n276: \t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"another scheduled pod was deleted, and it may make the unscheduled pod schedulable\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"deletedPod\", klog.KObj(originalPod))\n277: \t\treturn framework.Queue, nil\n278: \t}\n279: \n280: \tif !f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling {\n281: \t\t// If InPlacePodVerticalScaling (KEP 1287) is disabled, it cannot free up resources.\n282: \t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"another pod was modified, but InPlacePodVerticalScaling is disabled, so it doesn't make the unscheduled pod schedulable\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"modifiedPod\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod))\n283: \t\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\n284: \t}\n285: \n286: \t// Modifications may or may not be relevant. We only care about modifications that\n287: \t// change the other pod's resource request and the resource is also requested by the\n288: \t// pod we are trying to schedule.\n289: \tif !f.isResourceScaleDown(pod, originalPod, modifiedPod) {\n290: \t\tif loggerV := logger.V(10); loggerV.Enabled() {\n291: \t\t\t// Log more information.\n292: \t\t\tloggerV.Info(\"another Pod got modified, but the modification isn't related to the resource request\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"modifiedPod\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod), \"diff\", cmp.Diff(originalPod, modifiedPod))\n293: \t\t} else {\n294: \t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"another Pod got modified, but the modification isn't related to the resource request\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"modifiedPod\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod))\n295: \t\t}\n296: \t\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\n297: \t}\n298: \n299: \tlogger.V(5).Info(\"the max request resources of another scheduled pod got reduced and it may make the unscheduled pod schedulable\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"modifiedPod\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod))\n300: \treturn framework.Queue, nil\n301: }\n302: \n303: // isResourceScaleDown checks whether the resource request of the modified pod is less than the original pod\n304: // for the resources requested by the pod we are trying to schedule.\n305: func (f *Fit) isResourceScaleDown(targetPod, originalOtherPod, modifiedOtherPod *v1.Pod) bool {\n306: \tif modifiedOtherPod.Spec.NodeName == \"\" {\n307: \t\t// no resource is freed up whatever the pod is modified.\n308: \t\treturn false\n309: \t}\n310: \n311: \t// the other pod was scheduled, so modification or deletion may free up some resources.\n312: \toriginalMaxResourceReq, modifiedMaxResourceReq := &framework.Resource{}, &framework.Resource{}\n313: \toriginalMaxResourceReq.SetMaxResource(resource.PodRequests(originalOtherPod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled: f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling}))\n314: \tmodifiedMaxResourceReq.SetMaxResource(resource.PodRequests(modifiedOtherPod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled: f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling}))\n315: \n316: \t// check whether the resource request of the modified pod is less than the original pod.\n317: \tpodRequests := resource.PodRequests(targetPod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled: f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling})\n318: \tfor rName, rValue := range podRequests {\n319: \t\tif rValue.IsZero() {\n320: \t\t\t// We only care about the resources requested by the pod we are trying to schedule.\n321: \t\t\tcontinue\n322: \t\t}\n323: \t\tswitch rName {\n324: \t\tcase v1.ResourceCPU:\n325: \t\t\tif originalMaxResourceReq.MilliCPU > modifiedMaxResourceReq.MilliCPU {\n326: \t\t\t\treturn true\n327: \t\t\t}\n328: \t\tcase v1.ResourceMemory:\n329: \t\t\tif originalMaxResourceReq.Memory > modifiedMaxResourceReq.Memory {\n330: \t\t\t\treturn true\n331: \t\t\t}\n332: \t\tcase v1.ResourceEphemeralStorage:\n333: \t\t\tif originalMaxResourceReq.EphemeralStorage > modifiedMaxResourceReq.EphemeralStorage {\n334: \t\t\t\treturn true\n335: \t\t\t}\n336: \t\tdefault:\n337: \t\t\tif schedutil.IsScalarResourceName(rName) && originalMaxResourceReq.ScalarResources[rName] > modifiedMaxResourceReq.ScalarResources[rName] {\n338: \t\t\t\treturn true\n339: \t\t\t}\n340: \t\t}\n341: \t}\n342: \treturn false\n343: }\n344: \n345: // isSchedulableAfterNodeChange is invoked whenever a node added or changed. It checks whether\n346: // that change made a previously unschedulable pod schedulable.\n347: func (f *Fit) isSchedulableAfterNodeChange(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, oldObj, newObj interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\n348: \t_, modifiedNode, err := schedutil.As[*v1.Node](oldObj, newObj)\n349: \tif err != nil {\n350: \t\treturn framework.Queue, err\n351: \t}\n352: \t// TODO: also check if the original node meets the pod's resource requestments once preCheck is completely removed.\n353: \t// See: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/110175\n354: \tif isFit(pod, modifiedNode) {\n355: \t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"node was updated, and may fit with the pod's resource requestments\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(modifiedNode))\n356: \t\treturn framework.Queue, nil\n357: \t}\n358: \n359: \tlogger.V(5).Info(\"node was created or updated, but it doesn't have enough resource(s) to accommodate this pod\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(modifiedNode))\n360: \treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\n361: }\n362: \n363: // isFit checks if the pod fits the node. If the node is nil, it returns false.\n364: // It constructs a fake NodeInfo object for the node and checks if the pod fits the node.\n365: func isFit(pod *v1.Pod, node *v1.Node) bool {\n366: \tif invoked at the filter extension point.\n375: // Checks if a node has sufficient resources, such as cpu, memory, gpu, opaque int resources etc to run a pod.\n376: // It returns a list of insufficient resources, if empty, then the node has all the resources requested by the pod.\n377: func (f *Fit) Filter(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n378: \ts, err := getPreFilterState(cycleState)\n379: \tif err != nil {\n380: \t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n381: \t}\n382: \n383: \tinsufficientResources }\n404: \n405: // InsufficientResource describes what kind of resource limit is hit and caused the pod to not fit the node.\n406: type InsufficientResource struct {\n407: \tResourceName v1.ResourceName\n408: \t// We explicitly have a parameter for reason to avoid formatting a message on the fly\n409: \t// for common resources, which is expensive for cluster autoscaler fitsRequest(podRequest *preFilterState, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo, ignoredExtendedResources, ignoredResourceGroups sets.Set[string]) []InsufficientResource {\n422: \tinsufficientResources := make([]InsufficientResource, 0, 4)\n423: \n424: \tallowedPodNumber := nodeInfo.Allocatable.AllowedPodNumber\n425: \tif len(nodeInfo.Pods)+1 > allowedPodNumber {\n426: \t\tinsufficientResources = podRequest.ScalarResources {\n472: \t\t// Skip in case request quantity is zero\n473: \t\tif rQuant == 0 {\n474: \t\t\tcontinue\n475: \t\t}\n476: \n477: \t\tif v1helper.IsExtendedResourceName(rName) {\n478: \t\t\t// If this resource is one of the extended resources that should be ignored, we will skip checking it.\n479: \t\t\t// rName is guaranteed to have a slash due to API validation.\n480: (nodeInfo.Allocatable.ScalarResources[rName] - nodeInfo.Requested.ScalarResources[rName]) {\n490: \t\t\tinsufficientResources = append(insufficientResources, InsufficientResource{\n491: insufficientResources\n501: }\n502: \n503: // Score invoked at the Score extension point.\n504: func (f *Fit) Score(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) \\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n39: \\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/apis/config\\\"\\n40: \\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework/parallelize\\\"\\n41: )\\n42: \\n43: // NodeScoreList declares a list of nodes and their scores.\\n44: type NodeScoreList []NodeScore\\n45: \\n46: // NodeScore is a struct with node name and score.\\n47: type NodeScore struct {\\n48: \\tName  string\\n49: \\tScore int64\\n50: }\\n51: \\n52: // NodeToStatusMap declares map from node name to its status.\\n53: type NodeToStatusMap map[string]*Status\\n54: \\n55: // NodePluginScores is a struct with node name and scores for that node.\\n56: type NodePluginScores struct {\\n57: \\t// Name is node name.\\n58: \\tName string\\n59: \\t// Scores is scores from plugins and extenders.\\n60: \\tScores []PluginScore\\n61: \\t// TotalScore is the total score in Scores.\\n62: \\tTotalScore int64\\n63: }\\n64: \\n65: // PluginScore is a struct with plugin/extender name and score.\\n66: type PluginScore struct {\\n67: \\t// Name is the name of plugin or extender.\\n68: \\tName  string\\n69: \\tScore int64\\n70: }\\n71: \\n72: // Code is the Status code/type which is returned from plugins.\\n73: type Code int\\n74: \\n75: // These are predefined codes used in a Status.\\n76: // Note: when you add a new status, you have to add it in `codes` slice below.\\n77: const (\\n78: \\t// Success means that plugin ran correctly and found pod schedulable.\\n79: \\t// NOTE: A nil status is also considered as \\\"Success\\\".\\n80: \\tSuccess Code = iota\\n81: \\t// Error is one of the failures, used for internal plugin errors, unexpected input, etc.\\n82: \\t// Plugin shouldn't return this code for expected failures, like Unschedulable.\\n83: \\t// Since it's the unexpected failure, the scheduling queue registers the pod without unschedulable plugins.\\n84: \\t// Meaning, the Pod will be requeued to activeQ/backoffQ soon.\\n85: \\tError\\n86: \\t// Unschedulable is one of the failures, used when a plugin finds a pod unschedulable.\\n87: \\t// If it's returned from PreFilter or Filter, the scheduler might attempt to\\n88: \\t// run other postFilter plugins like preemption to get this pod scheduled.\\n89: \\t// Use UnschedulableAndUnresolvable to make the scheduler skipping other postFilter plugins.\\n90: \\t// The accompanying status message should explain why the pod is unschedulable.\\n91: \\t//\\n92: \\t// We regard the backoff as a penalty of wasting the scheduling cycle.\\n93: \\t// When the scheduling queue requeues Pods, which was rejected with Unschedulable in the last scheduling,\\n94: \\t// the Pod goes through backoff.\\n95: \\tUnschedulable\\n96: \\t// UnschedulableAndUnresolvable is used when a plugin finds a pod unschedulable and\\n97: \\t// other postFilter plugins like preemption would not change anything.\\n98: \\t// See the comment on PostFilter interface for more details about how PostFilter should handle this status.\\n99: \\t// Plugins should return Unschedulable if it is possible that the pod can get scheduled\\n100: \\t// after running other postFilter plugins.\\n101: \\t// The accompanying status message should explain why the pod is unschedulable.\\n102: \\t//\\n103: \\t// We regard the backoff as a penalty of wasting the scheduling cycle.\\n104: \\t// When the scheduling queue requeues Pods, which was rejected with UnschedulableAndUnresolvable in the last scheduling,\\n105: \\t// the Pod goes through backoff.\\n106: \\tUnschedulableAndUnresolvable\\n107: \\t// Wait is used when a Permit plugin finds a pod scheduling should wait.\\n108: \\tWait\\n109: \\t// Skip is used in the following scenarios:\\n110: \\t// - when a Bind plugin chooses to skip binding.\\n111: \\t// - when a PreFilter plugin returns Skip so that coupled Filter plugin/PreFilterExtensions() will be skipped.\\n112: \\t// - when a PreScore plugin returns Skip so that coupled Score plugin will be skipped.\\n113: \\tSkip\\n114: \\t// Pending means that the scheduling process is finished successfully,\\n115: \\t// but the plugin wants to stop the scheduling cycle/binding cycle here.\\n116: \\t//\\n117: \\t// For example, the DRA plugin sometimes needs to wait for the external device driver\\n118: \\t// to provision the resource for the Pod.\\n119: \\t// It's different from when to return Unschedulable/UnschedulableAndUnresolvable,\\n120: \\t// because in this case, the scheduler decides where the Pod can go successfully,\\n121: \\t// but we need to wait for the external component to do something based on that scheduling result.\\n122: \\t//\\n123: \\t// We regard the backoff as a penalty of wasting the scheduling cycle.\\n124: \\t// In the case of returning Pending, we cannot say the scheduling cycle is wasted\\n125: \\t// because the scheduling result is used to proceed the Pod's scheduling forward,\\n126: \\t// that particular scheduling cycle is failed though.\\n127: \\t// So, Pods rejected by such reasons don't need to suffer a penalty (backoff).\\n128: \\t// When the scheduling queue requeues Pods, which was rejected with Pending in the last scheduling,\\n129: \\t// the Pod goes to activeQ directly ignoring backoff.\\n130: \\tPending\\n131: )\\n132: \\n133: // This list should be exactly the same as the codes iota defined above in the same order.\\n134: var codes = []string{\\\"Success\\\", \\\"Error\\\", \\\"Unschedulable\\\", \\\"UnschedulableAndUnresolvable\\\", \\\"Wait\\\", \\\"Skip\\\", \\\"Pending\\\"}\\n135: \\n136: func (c Code) String() string {\\n137: \\treturn codes[c]\\n138: }\\n139: \\n140: const (\\n141: \\t// MaxNodeScore is the maximum score a Score plugin is expected to return.\\n142: \\tMaxNodeScore int64 = 100\\n143: \\n144: \\t// MinNodeScore is the minimum score a Score plugin is expected to return.\\n145: \\tMinNodeScore int64 = 0\\n146: \\n147: \\t// MaxTotalScore is the maximum total score.\\n148: \\tMaxTotalScore int64 = math.MaxInt64\\n149: )\\n150: \\n151: // PodsToActivateKey is a reserved state key for stashing pods.\\n152: // If the stashed pods are present in unschedulablePods or backoffQ，they will be\\n153: // activated (i.e., moved to activeQ) in two phases:\\n154: // - end of a scheduling cycle if it succeeds (will be cleared from `PodsToActivate` if activated)\\n155: // - end of a binding cycle if it succeeds\\n156: var PodsToActivateKey StateKey = \\\"kubernetes.io/pods-to-activate\\\"\\n157: \\n158: // PodsToActivate stores pods to be activated.\\n159: type PodsToActivate struct {\\n160: \\tsync.Mutex\\n161: \\t// Map is keyed with namespaced pod name, and valued with the pod.\\n162: \\tMap map[string]*v1.Pod\\n163: }\\n164: \\n165: // Clone just returns the same state.\\n166: func (s *PodsToActivate) Clone() StateData {\\n167: \\treturn s\\n168: }\\n169: \\n170: // NewPodsToActivate instantiates a PodsToActivate object.\\n171: func NewPodsToActivate() *PodsToActivate {\\n172: \\treturn &PodsToActivate{Map: make(map[string]*v1.Pod)}\\n173: }\\n174: \\n175: // Status indicates the result of running a plugin. It consists of a code, a\\n176: // message, (optionally) an error, and a plugin name it fails by.\\n177: // When the status code is not Success, the reasons should explain why.\\n178: // And, when code is Success, all the other fields should be empty.\\n179: // NOTE: A nil Status is also considered as Success.\\n180: type Status struct {\\n181: \\tcode    Code\\n182: \\treasons []string\\n183: \\terr     error\\n184: \\t// plugin is an optional field that records the plugin name causes this status.\\n185: \\t// It's set by the framework when code is Unschedulable, UnschedulableAndUnresolvable or Pending.\\n186: \\tplugin string\\n187: }\\n188: \\n189: func (s *Status) WithError(err error) *Status {\\n190: \\ts.err = err\\n191: \\treturn s\\n192: }\\n193: \\n194: // Code returns code of the Status.\\n195: func (s *Status) Code() Code {\\n196: \\tif s == nil {\\n197: \\t\\treturn Success\\n198: \\t}\\n199: \\treturn s.code\\n200: }\\n201: \\n202: // Message returns a concatenated message on reasons of the Status.\\n203: func (s *Status) Message() string {\\n204: \\tif s == nil {\\n205: \\t\\treturn \\\"\\\"\\n206: \\t}\\n207: \\treturn strings.Join(s.Reasons(), \\\", \\\")\\n208: }\\n209: \\n210: // SetPlugin sets the given plugin name to s.plugin.\\n211: func (s *Status) SetPlugin(plugin string) {\\n212: \\ts.plugin = plugin\\n213: }\\n214: \\n215: // WithPlugin sets the given plugin name to s.plugin,\\n216: // and returns the given status object.\\n217: func (s *Status) WithPlugin(plugin string) *Status {\\n218: \\ts.SetPlugin(plugin)\\n219: \\treturn s\\n220: }\\n221: \\n222: // Plugin returns the plugin name which caused this status.\\n223: func (s *Status) Plugin() string {\\n224: \\treturn s.plugin\\n225: }\\n226: \\n227: // Reasons returns reasons of the Status.\\n228: func (s *Status) Reasons() []string {\\n229: \\tif s.err != nil {\\n230: \\t\\treturn append([]string{s.err.Error()}, s.reasons...)\\n231: \\t}\\n232: \\treturn s.reasons\\n233: }\\n234: \\n235: // AppendReason appends given reason to the Status.\\n236: func (s *Status) AppendReason(reason string) {\\n237: \\ts.reasons = append(s.reasons, reason)\\n238: }\\n239: \\n240: // IsSuccess returns true if and only if \\\"Status\\\" is nil or Code is \\\"Success\\\".\\n241: func (s *Status) IsSuccess() bool {\\n242: \\treturn s.Code() == Success\\n243: }\\n244: \\n245: // IsWait returns true if and only if \\\"Status\\\" is non-nil and its Code is \\\"Wait\\\".\\n246: func (s *Status) IsWait() bool {\\n247: \\treturn s.Code() == Wait\\n248: }\\n249: \\n250: // IsSkip returns true if and only if \\\"Status\\\" is non-nil and its Code is \\\"Skip\\\".\\n251: func (s *Status) IsSkip() bool {\\n252: \\treturn s.Code() == Skip\\n253: }\\n254: \\n255: // IsRejected returns true if \\\"Status\\\" is Unschedulable (Unschedulable, UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, or Pending).\\n256: func (s *Status) IsRejected() bool {\\n257: \\tcode := s.Code()\\n258: \\treturn code == Unschedulable || code == UnschedulableAndUnresolvable || code == Pending\\n259: }\\n260: \\n261: // AsError returns nil if the status is a success, a wait or a skip; otherwise returns an \\\"error\\\" object\\n262: // with a concatenated message on reasons of the Status.\\n263: func (s *Status) AsError() error {\\n264: \\tif s.IsSuccess() || s.IsWait() || s.IsSkip() {\\n265: \\t\\treturn nil\\n266: \\t}\\n267: \\tif s.err != nil {\\n268: \\t\\treturn s.err\\n269: \\t}\\n270: \\treturn errors.New(s.Message())\\n271: }\\n272: \\n273: // Equal checks equality of two statuses. This is useful for testing with\\n274: // cmp.Equal.\\n275: func (s *Status) Equal(x *Status) bool {\\n276: \\tif s == nil || x == nil {\\n277: \\t\\treturn s.IsSuccess() && x.IsSuccess()\\n278: \\t}\\n279: \\tif s.code != x.code {\\n280: \\t\\treturn false\\n281: \\t}\\n282: \\tif !cmp.Equal(s.err, x.err, cmpopts.EquateErrors()) {\\n283: \\t\\treturn false\\n284: \\t}\\n285: \\tif !cmp.Equal(s.reasons, x.reasons) {\\n286: \\t\\treturn false\\n287: \\t}\\n288: \\treturn cmp.Equal(s.plugin, x.plugin)\\n289: }\\n290: \\n291: func (s *Status) String() string {\\n292: \\treturn s.Message()\\n293: }\\n294: \\n295: // NewStatus makes a Status out of the given arguments and returns its pointer.\\n296: func NewStatus(code Code, reasons ...string) *Status {\\n297: \\ts := &Status{\\n298: \\t\\tcode:    code,\\n299: \\t\\treasons: reasons,\\n300: \\t}\\n301: \\treturn s\\n302: }\\n303: \\n304: // AsStatus wraps an error in a Status.\\n305: func AsStatus(err error) *Status {\\n306: \\tif err == nil {\\n307: \\t\\treturn nil\\n308: \\t}\\n309: \\treturn &Status{\\n310: \\t\\tcode: Error,\\n311: \\t\\terr:  err,\\n312: \\t}\\n313: }\\n314: \\n315: // WaitingPod represents a pod currently waiting in the permit phase.\\n316: type WaitingPod interface {\\n317: \\t// GetPod returns a reference to the waiting pod.\\n318: \\tGetPod() *v1.Pod\\n319: \\t// GetPendingPlugins returns a list of pending Permit plugin's name.\\n320: \\tGetPendingPlugins() []string\\n321: \\t// Allow declares the waiting pod is allowed to be scheduled by the plugin named as \\\"pluginName\\\".\\n322: \\t// If this is the last remaining plugin to allow, then a success signal is delivered\\n323: \\t// to unblock the pod.\\n324: \\tAllow(pluginName string)\\n325: \\t// Reject declares the waiting pod unschedulable.\\n326: \\tReject(pluginName, msg string)\\n327: }\\n328: \\n329: // Plugin is the parent type for all the scheduling framework plugins.\\n330: type Plugin interface {\\n331: \\tName() string\\n332: }\\n333: \\n334: // PreEnqueuePlugin is an interface that must be implemented by \\\"PreEnqueue\\\" plugins.\\n335: // These plugins are called prior to adding Pods to activeQ.\\n336: // Note: an preEnqueue plugin is expected to be lightweight and efficient, so it's not expected to\\n337: // involve expensive calls like accessing external endpoints; otherwise it'd block other\\n338: // Pods' enqueuing in event handlers.\\n339: type PreEnqueuePlugin interface {\\n340: \\tPlugin\\n341: \\t// PreEnqueue is called prior to adding Pods to activeQ.\\n342: \\tPreEnqueue(ctx context.Context, p *v1.Pod) *Status\\n343: }\\n344: \\n345: // LessFunc is the function to sort pod info\\n346: type LessFunc func(podInfo1, podInfo2 *QueuedPodInfo) bool\\n347: \\n348: // QueueSortPlugin is an interface that must be implemented by \\\"QueueSort\\\" plugins.\\n349: // These plugins are used to sort pods in the scheduling queue. Only one queue sort\\n350: // plugin may be enabled at a time.\\n351: type QueueSortPlugin interface {\\n352: \\tPlugin\\n353: \\t// Less are used to sort pods in the scheduling queue.\\n354: \\tLess(*QueuedPodInfo, *QueuedPodInfo) bool\\n355: }\\n356: \\n357: // EnqueueExtensions is an optional interface that plugins can implement to efficiently\\n358: // move unschedulable Pods in internal scheduling queues.\\n359: // In the scheduler, Pods can be unschedulable by PreEnqueue, PreFilter, Filter, Reserve, and Permit plugins,\\n360: // and Pods rejected by these plugins are requeued based on this extension point.\\n361: // Failures from other extension points are regarded as temporal errors (e.g., network failure),\\n362: // and the scheduler requeue Pods without this extension point - always requeue Pods to activeQ after backoff.\\n363: // This is because such temporal errors cannot be resolved by specific cluster events,\\n364: // and we have no choise but keep retrying scheduling until the failure is resolved.\\n365: //\\n366: // Plugins that make pod unschedulable (PreEnqueue, PreFilter, Filter, Reserve, and Permit plugins) should implement this interface,\\n367: // otherwise the default implementation will be used, which is less efficient in requeueing Pods rejected by the plugin.\\n368: // And, if plugins other than above extension points support this interface, they are just ignored.\\n369: type EnqueueExtensions interface {\\n370: \\tPlugin\\n371: \\t// EventsToRegister returns a series of possible events that may cause a Pod\\n372: \\t// failed by this plugin schedulable. Each event has a callback function that\\n373: \\t// filters out events to reduce useless retry of Pod's scheduling.\\n374: \\t// The events will be registered when instantiating the internal scheduling queue,\\n375: \\t// and leveraged to build event handlers dynamically.\\n376: \\t// Note: the returned list needs to be static (not depend on configuration parameters);\\n377: \\t// otherwise it would lead to undefined behavior.\\n378: \\t//\\n379: \\t// Appropriate implementation of this function will make Pod's re-scheduling accurate and performant.\\n380: \\tEventsToRegister() []ClusterEventWithHint\\n381: }\\n382: \\n383: // PreFilterExtensions is an interface that is included in plugins that allow specifying\\n384: // callbacks to make incremental updates to its supposedly pre-calculated\\n385: // state.\\n386: type PreFilterExtensions interface {\\n387: \\t// AddPod is called by the framework while trying to evaluate the impact\\n388: \\t// of adding podToAdd to the node while scheduling podToSchedule.\\n389: \\tAddPod(ctx context.Context, state *CycleState, podToSchedule *v1.Pod, podInfoToAdd *PodInfo, nodeInfo *NodeInfo) *Status\\n390: \\t// RemovePod is called by the framework while trying to evaluate the impact\\n391: \\t// of removing podToRemove from the node while scheduling podToSchedule.\\n392: \\tRemovePod(ctx context.Context, state *CycleState, podToSchedule *v1.Pod, podInfoToRemove *PodInfo, nodeInfo *NodeInfo) *Status\\n393: }\\n394: \\n395: // PreFilterPlugin is an interface that must be implemented by \\\"PreFilter\\\" plugins.\\n396: // These plugins are called at the beginning of the scheduling cycle.\\n397: type PreFilterPlugin interface {\\n398: \\tPlugin\\n399: \\t// PreFilter is called at the beginning of the scheduling cycle. All PreFilter\\n400: \\t// plugins must return success or the pod will be rejected. PreFilter could optionally\\n401: \\t// return a PreFilterResult to influence which nodes to evaluate downstream. This is useful\\n402: \\t// for cases where it is possible to determine the subset of nodes to process in O(1) time.\\n403: \\t// When PreFilterResult filters out some Nodes, the framework considers Nodes that are filtered out as getting \\\"UnschedulableAndUnresolvable\\\".\\n404: \\t// i.e., those Nodes will be out of the candidates of the preemption.\\n405: \\t//\\n406: \\t// When it returns Skip status, returned PreFilterResult and other fields in status are just ignored,\\n407: \\t// and coupled Filter plugin/PreFilterExtensions() will be skipped in this scheduling cycle.\\n408: \\tPreFilter(ctx context.Context, state *CycleState, p *v1.Pod) (*PreFilterResult, *Status)\\n409: \\t// PreFilterExtensions returns a PreFilterExtensions interface if the plugin implements one,\\n410: \\t// or nil if it does not. A Pre-filter plugin can provide extensions to incrementally\\n411: \\t// modify its pre-processed info. The framework guarantees that the extensions\\n412: \\t// AddPod/RemovePod will only be called after PreFilter, possibly on a cloned\\n413: \\t// CycleState, and may call those functions more than once before calling\\n414: \\t// Filter again on a specific node.\\n415: \\tPreFilterExtensions() PreFilterExtensions\\n416: }\\n417: \\n418: // FilterPlugin is an interface for Filter plugins. These plugins are called at the\\n419: // filter extension point for filtering out hosts that cannot run a pod.\\n420: // This concept used to be called 'predicate' in the original scheduler.\\n421: // These plugins should return \\\"Success\\\", \\\"Unschedulable\\\" or \\\"Error\\\" in Status.code.\\n422: // However, the scheduler accepts other valid codes as well.\\n423: // Anything other than \\\"Success\\\" will lead to exclusion of the given host from\\n424: // running the pod.\\n425: type FilterPlugin interface {\\n426: \\tPlugin\\n427: \\t// Filter is called by the scheduling framework.\\n428: \\t// All FilterPlugins should return \\\"Success\\\" to declare that\\n429: \\t// the given node fits the pod. If Filter doesn't return \\\"Success\\\",\\n430: \\t// it will return \\\"Unschedulable\\\", \\\"UnschedulableAndUnresolvable\\\" or \\\"Error\\\".\\n431: \\t// For the node being evaluated, Filter plugins should look at the passed\\n432: \\t// nodeInfo reference for this particular node's information (e.g., pods\\n433: \\t// considered to be running on the node) instead of looking it up in the\\n434: \\t// NodeInfoSnapshot because we don't guarantee that they will be the same.\\n435: \\t// For example, during preemption, we may pass a copy of the original\\n436: \\t// nodeInfo object that has some pods removed from it to evaluate the\\n437: \\t// possibility of preempting them to schedule the target pod.\\n438: \\tFilter(ctx context.Context, state *CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeInfo *NodeInfo) *Status\\n439: }\\n440: \\n441: // PostFilterPlugin is an interface for \\\"PostFilter\\\" plugins. These plugins are called\\n442: // after a pod cannot be scheduled.\\n443: type PostFilterPlugin interface {\\n444: \\tPlugin\\n445: \\t// PostFilter is called by the scheduling framework\\n446: \\t// when the scheduling cycle failed at PreFilter or Filter by Unschedulable or UnschedulableAndUnresolvable.\\n447: \\t// NodeToStatusMap has statuses that each Node got in the Filter phase.\\n448: \\t// If this scheduling cycle failed at PreFilter, all Nodes have the status from the rejector PreFilter plugin in NodeToStatusMap.\\n449: \\t// Note that the scheduling framework runs PostFilter plugins even when PreFilter returned UnschedulableAndUnresolvable.\\n450: \\t// In that case, NodeToStatusMap contains all Nodes with UnschedulableAndUnresolvable.\\n451: \\t//\\n452: \\t// Also, ignoring Nodes with UnschedulableAndUnresolvable is the responsibility of each PostFilter plugin,\\n453: \\t// meaning NodeToStatusMap obviously could have Nodes with UnschedulableAndUnresolvable\\n454: \\t// and the scheduling framework does call PostFilter even when all Nodes in NodeToStatusMap are UnschedulableAndUnresolvable.\\n455: \\t//\\n456: \\t// A PostFilter plugin should return one of the following statuses:\\n457: \\t// - Unschedulable: the plugin gets executed successfully but the pod cannot be made schedulable.\\n458: \\t// - Success: the plugin gets executed successfully and the pod can be made schedulable.\\n459: \\t// - Error: the plugin aborts due to some internal error.\\n460: \\t//\\n461: \\t// Informational plugins should be configured ahead of other ones, and always return Unschedulable status.\\n462: \\t// Optionally, a non-nil PostFilterResult may be returned along with a Success status. For example,\\n463: \\t// a preemption plugin may choose to return nominatedNodeName, so that framework can reuse that to update the\\n464: \\t// preemptor pod's .spec.status.nominatedNodeName field.\\n465: \\tPostFilter(ctx context.Context, state *CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, filteredNodeStatusMap NodeToStatusMap) (*PostFilterResult, *Status)\\n466: }\\n467: \\n468: // PreScorePlugin is an interface for \\\"PreScore\\\" plugin. PreScore is an\\n469: // informational extension point. Plugins will be called with a list of nodes\\n470: // that passed the filtering phase. A plugin may use this data to update internal\\n471: // state or to generate logs/metrics.\\n472: type PreScorePlugin interface {\\n473: \\tPlugin\\n474: \\t// PreScore is called by the scheduling framework after a list of nodes\\n475: \\t// passed the filtering phase. All prescore plugins must return success or\\n476: \\t// the pod will be rejected\\n477: \\t// When it returns Skip status, other fields in status are just ignored,\\n478: \\t// and coupled Score plugin will be skipped in this scheduling cycle.\\n479: \\tPreScore(ctx context.Context, state *CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodes []*NodeInfo) *Status\\n480: }\\n481: \\n482: // ScoreExtensions is an interface for Score extended functionality.\\n483: type ScoreExtensions interface {\\n484: \\t// NormalizeScore is called for all node scores produced by the same plugin's \\\"Score\\\"\\n485: \\t// method. These are meant to update the state of the plugin. This concept\\n505: // used to be called 'assume' in the original scheduler. These plugins should\\n506: // return only Success or Error in Status.code. However, the scheduler accepts\\n507: // other valid codes as well. Anything other than Success will lead to\\n508: // rejection of the pod.\\n509: type ReservePlugin interface {\\n510: \\tPlugin\\n511: \\t// Reserve is called by the scheduling framework when the scheduler cache is\\n512: \\t// updated. Bind\\n557: // plugins are used to bind a pod to a Node.\\n558: type BindPlugin interface {\\n559: \\tPlugin\\n560: \\t// Bind plugins will not be called until all pre-bind plugins have completed. Each\\n561: \\t// bind plugin is called in the configured order. A bind plugin may choose whether\\n562: \\t// or not to handle the given Pod. It returns directly if any of the plugins return any\\n816: \\t// status other than Success.\\n817: \\tRunPreFilterExtensionRemovePod(ctx context.Context, state *CycleState, podToSchedule *v1.Pod, podInfoToRemove *PodInfo, nodeInfo *NodeInfo) *Status\\n818: }\\n819: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/pkg/scheduler/framework/interface.go\"}"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"406cf5fd-01a6-4a11-af68-026811414216","uuid":"c9808846-86ef-4866-9d6b-9d07c41c4d3f","tool_use_result":{"content":"{\"content\":\"1:
