\\t\\t\\t\\t// We try to close all plugins even if we got errors from some.\\n422: \\t\\t\\t}\\n423: \\t\\t}\\n424: \\t}\\n425: \\treturn errors.Join(errs...)\\n426: }\\n427: \\n428: // getScoreWeights makes sure that, between MultiPoint-Score plugin weights and individual Score\\n429: // plugin weights there is not an overflow of MaxTotalScore.\\n430: func getScoreWeights(f *frameworkImpl, plugins []config.Plugin) error {\\n431: \\tvar totalPriority int64\\n432: \\tscorePlugins := reflect.ValueOf(&f.scorePlugins).Elem()\\n433: \\tpluginType := scorePlugins.Type().Elem()\\n434: \\tfor _, e := range plugins {\\n435: \\t\\tpg := f.pluginsMap[e.Name]\\n436: \\t\\tif !reflect.TypeOf(pg).Implements(pluginType) {\\n437: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n438: \\t\\t}\\n439: \\n440: \\t\\t// We append MultiPoint plugins to the list of Score plugins. So if this plugin has already been\\n441: \\t\\t// encountered, let the individual Score weight take precedence.\\n442: \\t\\tif _, ok := f.scorePluginWeight[e.Name]; ok {\\n443: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n444: \\t\\t}\\n445: \\t\\t// a weight of zero is not permitted, plugins can be disabled explicitly\\n446: \\t\\t// when configured.\\n447: \\t\\tf.scorePluginWeight[e.Name] = int(e.Weight)\\n448: \\t\\tif f.scorePluginWeight[e.Name] == 0 {\\n449: \\t\\t\\tf.scorePluginWeight[e.Name] = 1\\n450: \\t\\t}\\n451: \\n452: \\t\\t// Checks totalPriority against MaxTotalScore to avoid overflow\\n453: \\t\\tif int64(f.scorePluginWeight[e.Name])*framework.MaxNodeScore > framework.MaxTotalScore-totalPriority {\\n454: \\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"total score of Score plugins could overflow\\\")\\n455: \\t\\t}\\n456: \\t\\ttotalPriority += int64(f.scorePluginWeight[e.Name]) * framework.MaxNodeScore\\n457: \\t}\\n458: \\treturn nil\\n459: }\\n460: \\n461: type orderedSet struct {\\n462: \\tset         map[string]int\\n463: \\tlist        []string\\n464: \\tdeletionCnt int\\n465: }\\n466: \\n467: func newOrderedSet() *orderedSet {\\n468: \\treturn &orderedSet{set: make(map[string]int)}\\n469: }\\n470: \\n471: func (os *orderedSet) insert(s string) {\\n472: \\tif os.has(s) {\\n473: \\t\\treturn\\n474: \\t}\\n475: \\tos.set[s] = len(os.list)\\n476: \\tos.list = append(os.list, s)\\n477: }\\n478: \\n479: func (os *orderedSet) has(s string) bool {\\n480: \\t_, found := os.set[s]\\n481: \\treturn found\\n482: }\\n483: \\n484: func (os *orderedSet) delete(s string) {\\n485: \\tif i, found := os.set[s]; found {\\n486: \\t\\tdelete(os.set, s)\\n487: \\t\\tos.list = append(os.list[:i-os.deletionCnt], os.list[i+1-os.deletionCnt:]...)\\n488: \\t\\tos.deletionCnt++\\n489: \\t}\\n490: }\\n491: \\n492: func (f *frameworkImpl) expandMultiPointPlugins(logger klog.Logger, profile *config.KubeSchedulerProfile) error {\\n493: \\t// initialize MultiPoint plugins\\n494: \\tfor _, e := range f.getExtensionPoints(profile.Plugins) {\\n495: \\t\\tplugins := reflect.ValueOf(e.slicePtr).Elem()\\n496: \\t\\tpluginType := plugins.Type().Elem()\\n497: \\t\\t// build enabledSet of plugins already registered via normal extension points\\n498: \\t\\t// to check double registration\\n499: \\t\\tenabledSet := newOrderedSet()\\n500: \\t\\tfor _, plugin := range e.plugins.Enabled {\\n501: \\t\\t\\tenabledSet.insert(plugin.Name)\\n502: \\t\\t}\\n503: \\n504: \\t\\tdisabledSet := sets.New[string]()\\n505: \\t\\tfor _, disabledPlugin := range e.plugins.Disabled {\\n506: \\t\\t\\tdisabledSet.Insert(disabledPlugin.Name)\\n507: \\t\\t}\\n508: \\t\\tif disabledSet.Has(\\\"*\\\") {\\n509: \\t\\t\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"Skipped MultiPoint expansion because all plugins are disabled for extension point\\\", \\\"extension\\\", pluginType)\\n510: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n511: \\t\\t}\\n512: \\n513: \\t\\t// track plugins enabled via multipoint separately from those enabled by specific extensions,\\n514: \\t\\t// so that we can distinguish between double-registration and explicit overrides\\n515: \\t\\tmultiPointEnabled := newOrderedSet()\\n516: \\t\\toverridePlugins := newOrderedSet()\\n517: \\t\\tfor _, ep := range profile.Plugins.MultiPoint.Enabled {\\n518: \\t\\t\\tpg, ok := f.pluginsMap[ep.Name]\\n519: \\t\\t\\tif !ok {\\n520: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"%s %q does not exist\\\", pluginType.Name(), ep.Name)\\n521: \\t\\t\\t}\\n522: \\n523: \\t\\t\\t// if this plugin doesn't implement the type for the current extension we're trying to expand, skip\\n524: \\t\\t\\tif !reflect.TypeOf(pg).Implements(pluginType) {\\n525: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n526: \\t\\t\\t}\\n527: \\n528: \\t\\t\\t// a plugin that's enabled via MultiPoint can still be disabled for specific extension points\\n529: \\t\\t\\tif disabledSet.Has(ep.Name) {\\n530: \\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"Skipped disabled plugin for extension point\\\", \\\"plugin\\\", ep.Name, \\\"extension\\\", pluginType)\\n531: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n532: \\t\\t\\t}\\n533: \\n534: \\t\\t\\t// if this plugin has already been enabled by the specific extension point,\\n535: \\t\\t\\t// the user intent is to override the default plugin or make some other explicit setting.\\n536: \\t\\t\\t// Either way, discard the MultiPoint value for this plugin.\\n537: \\t\\t\\t// This maintains expected behavior for overriding default plugins (see https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/99582)\\n538: \\t\\t\\tif enabledSet.has(ep.Name) {\\n539: \\t\\t\\t\\toverridePlugins.insert(ep.Name)\\n540: \\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"MultiPoint plugin is explicitly re-configured; overriding\\\", \\\"plugin\\\", ep.Name)\\n541: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n542: \\t\\t\\t}\\n543: \\n544: \\t\\t\\t// if this plugin is already registered via MultiPoint, then this is\\n545: \\t\\t\\t// a double registration and an error in the config.\\n546: \\t\\t\\tif multiPointEnabled.has(ep.Name) {\\n547: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"plugin %q already registered as %q\\\", ep.Name, pluginType.Name())\\n548: \\t\\t\\t}\\n549: \\n550: \\t\\t\\t// we only need to update the multipoint set, since we already have the specific extension set from above\\n551: \\t\\t\\tmultiPointEnabled.insert(ep.Name)\\n552: \\t\\t}\\n553: \\n554: \\t\\t// Reorder plugins. Here is the expected order:\\n555: \\t\\t// - part 1: overridePlugins. Their order stay intact as how they're specified in regular extension point.\\n556: \\t\\t// - part 2: multiPointEnabled - i.e., plugin defined in multipoint but not in regular extension point.\\n557: \\t\\t// - part 3: other plugins (excluded by part 1 & 2) in regular extension point.\\n558: \\t\\tnewPlugins := reflect.New(reflect.TypeOf(e.slicePtr).Elem()).Elem()\\n559: \\t\\t// part 1\\n560: \\t\\tfor _, name := range slice.CopyStrings(enabledSet.list) {\\n561: \\t\\t\\tif overridePlugins.has(name) {\\n562: \\t\\t\\t\\tnewPlugins = reflect.Append(newPlugins, reflect.ValueOf(f.pluginsMap[name]))\\n563: \\t\\t\\t\\tenabledSet.delete(name)\\n564: \\t\\t\\t}\\n565: \\t\\t}\\n566: \\t\\t// part 2\\n567: \\t\\tfor _, name := range multiPointEnabled.list {\\n568: \\t\\t\\tnewPlugins = reflect.Append(newPlugins, reflect.ValueOf(f.pluginsMap[name]))\\n569: \\t\\t}\\n570: \\t\\t// part 3\\n571: \\t\\tfor _, name := range enabledSet.list {\\n572: \\t\\t\\tnewPlugins = reflect.Append(newPlugins, reflect.ValueOf(f.pluginsMap[name]))\\n573: \\t\\t}\\n574: \\t\\tplugins.Set(newPlugins)\\n575: \\t}\\n576: \\treturn nil\\n577: }\\n578: \\n579: func shouldHaveEnqueueExtensions(p framework.Plugin) bool {\\n580: \\tswitch p.(type) {\\n581: \\t// Only PreEnqueue, PreFilter, Filter, Reserve, and Permit plugins can (should) have EnqueueExtensions.\\n582: \\t// See the comment of EnqueueExtensions for more detailed reason here.\\n583: \\tcase framework.PreEnqueuePlugin, framework.PreFilterPlugin, framework.FilterPlugin, framework.ReservePlugin, framework.PermitPlugin:\\n584: \\t\\treturn true\\n585: \\t}\\n586: \\treturn false\\n587: }\\n588: \\n589: func (f *frameworkImpl) fillEnqueueExtensions(p framework.Plugin) They correspond to a reasonable size range for\\n31: // container images compressed and stored in registries; 90%ile of images on dockerhub drops into this range.\\n32: const (\\n33: \\tmb                    int64 = 1024 * 1024\\n34: \\tminThreshold          int64 = 23 * mb\\n35: \\tmaxContainerThreshold int64 = 1000 * mb\\n36: )\\n37: \\n38: // ImageLocality is a score plugin that favors nodes that already have requested pod container's images.\\n39: type ImageLocality struct {\\n40: \\thandle framework.Handle\\n41: }\\n42: \\n43: var _ framework.ScorePlugin = &ImageLocality{}\\n44: \\n45: // Name is the name of the plugin used in the plugin registry and configurations.\\n46: const Name = names.ImageLocality\\n47: \\n48: // Name returns name of the plugin. Given the sumScores of requested images on the node, the node's\\n83: // priority is obtained by scaling the maximum priority value with a ratio proportional to the sumScores.\\n84: func calculatePriority(sumScores int64, numContainers int) int64 {\\n85: \\tmaxThreshold := maxContainerThreshold * int64(numContainers)\\n86: \\tif sumScores < minThreshold {\\n87: \\t\\tsumScores = minThreshold\\n88: \\t} else if sumScores > maxThreshold {\\n89: \\t\\tsumScores = maxThreshold\\n90: \\t}\\n91: \\n92: \\treturn framework.MaxNodeScore * (sumScores - minThreshold) / (maxThreshold - minThreshold)\\n93: }\\n94: \\n95: // sumImageScores returns the sum of image scores of all the containers that are already on the node.\\n96: // Each image receives a raw score of its size, scaled by scaledImageScore. It is used in logs, etc.\\n146: func (f *Fit) Name() string {\\n147: \\treturn Name\\n148: }\\n149: \\n150: // NewFit initializes a new plugin and returns it.\\n151: func NewFit(_ context.Context, plArgs runtime.Object, h framework.Handle, fts feature.Features) (framework.Plugin, error) {\\n152: \\targs, ok := plArgs.(*config.NodeResourcesFitArgs)\\n153: \\tif !ok {\\n154: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"want args to be of type NodeResourcesFitArgs, got %T\\\", plArgs)\\n155: \\t}\\n156: \\tif err := validation.ValidateNodeResourcesFitArgs(nil, args); err != nil {\\n157: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n158: \\t}\\n159: \\n160: \\tif args.ScoringStrategy == nil {\\n161: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"scoring strategy not specified\\\")\\n162: \\t}\\n163: \\n164: \\tstrategy := args.ScoringStrategy.Type\\n165: \\tscorePlugin, exists := nodeResourceStrategyTypeMap[strategy]\\n166: \\tif !exists {\\n167: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"scoring strategy %s is not supported\\\", strategy)\\n168: \\t}\\n169: \\n170: \\treturn &Fit{\\n171: \\t\\tignoredResources:                sets.New(args.IgnoredResources...),\\n172: \\t\\tignoredResourceGroups:           sets.New(args.IgnoredResourceGroups...),\\n173: \\t\\tenableInPlacePodVerticalScaling: fts.EnableInPlacePodVerticalScaling,\\n174: \\t\\tenableSidecarContainers:         fts.EnableSidecarContainers,\\n175: \\t\\thandle:                          h,\\n176: \\t\\tresourceAllocationScorer:        *scorePlugin(args),\\n177: \\t}, nil\\n178: }\\n179: \\n180: // computePodResourceRequest returns a framework.Resource that covers the largest\\n181: // width in each resource dimension. Because init-containers run sequentially, we collect\\n182: // the max in each dimension iteratively. In contrast, we sum the resource vectors for\\n183: // regular containers since they run simultaneously.\\n184: //\\n185: // # The resources defined for Overhead should be added to the calculated Resource request sum\\n186: //\\n187: // Example:\\n188: //\\n189: // Pod:\\n190: //\\n191: //\\tInitContainers\\n192: //\\t  IC1:\\n193: //\\t    CPU: 2\\n194: //\\t    Memory: 1G\\n195: //\\t  IC2:\\n196: //\\t    CPU: 2\\n197: //\\t    Memory: 3G\\n198: //\\tContainers\\n199: //\\t  C1:\\n200: //\\t    CPU: 2\\n201: //\\t    Memory: 1G\\n202: //\\t  C2:\\n203: //\\t    CPU: 1\\n204: //\\t    Memory: 1G\\n205: //\\n206: // Result: CPU: 3, Memory: 3G\\n207: func computePodResourceRequest(pod *v1.Pod) *preFilterState {\\n208: \\t// pod hasn't scheduled yet so we don't need to worry about InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled\\n209: \\treqs := resource.PodRequests(pod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{})\\n210: \\tresult := &preFilterState{}\\n211: \\tresult.SetMaxResource(reqs)\\n212: \\treturn result\\n213: }\\n214: \\n215: // PreFilter invoked at the prefilter extension point.\\n216: func (f *Fit) PreFilter(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (*framework.PreFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\\n217: \\tif !f.enableSidecarContainers && hasRestartableInitContainer(pod) {\\n218: \\t\\t// Scheduler will calculate resources usage for a Pod containing\\n219: \\t\\t// restartable init containers that will be equal or more than kubelet will\\n220: \\t\\t// require to run the Pod. So there will be no overbooking. However, to\\n221: \\t\\t// avoid the inconsistency in resource calculation between the scheduler\\n222: \\t\\t// and the older (before v1.28) kubelet, make the Pod unschedulable.\\n223: \\t\\treturn nil, framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, \\\"Pod has a restartable init container and the SidecarContainers feature is disabled\\\")\\n224: \\t}\\n225: \\tcycleState.Write(preFilterStateKey, computePodResourceRequest(pod))\\n226: \\treturn nil, nil\\n227: }\\n228: \\n229: // PreFilterExtensions returns prefilter extensions, pod add and remove.\\n230: func (f *Fit) PreFilterExtensions() framework.PreFilterExtensions {\\n231: \\treturn nil\\n232: }\\n233: \\n234: func getPreFilterState(cycleState *framework.CycleState) (*preFilterState, error) {\\n235: \\tc, err := cycleState.Read(preFilterStateKey)\\n236: \\tif err != nil {\\n237: \\t\\t// preFilterState doesn't exist, likely PreFilter wasn't invoked.\\n238: \\t\\treturn nil, updated. It checks whether\\n264: // that change made a previously unschedulable pod schedulable.\\n265: func (f *Fit) isSchedulableAfterPodChange(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, oldObj, newObj interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\\n266: \\toriginalPod, modifiedPod, err := schedutil.As[*v1.Pod](oldObj, newObj)\\n267: \\tif err != nil {\\n268: \\t\\treturn framework.Queue, err\\n269: \\t}\\n270: \\n271: \\tif modifiedPod == nil {\\n272: \\t\\tif originalPod.Spec.NodeName == \\\"\\\" {\\n273: \\t\\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"the deleted pod was unscheduled and it wouldn't make the unscheduled pod schedulable\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"deletedPod\\\", klog.KObj(originalPod))\\n274: \\t\\t\\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\\n275: \\t\\t}\\n276: \\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"another scheduled pod was deleted, and it may make the unscheduled pod schedulable\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"deletedPod\\\", klog.KObj(originalPod))\\n277: \\t\\treturn framework.Queue, nil\\n278: \\t}\\n279: \\n280: \\tif !f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling {\\n281: \\t\\t// If InPlacePodVerticalScaling (KEP 1287) is disabled, it cannot free up resources.\\n282: \\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"another pod was modified, but InPlacePodVerticalScaling is disabled, so it doesn't make the unscheduled pod schedulable\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"modifiedPod\\\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod))\\n283: \\t\\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\\n284: \\t}\\n285: \\n286: \\t// Modifications may or may not be relevant. }\\n302: \\n303: // isResourceScaleDown checks whether the resource request of the modified pod is less than the original pod\\n304: // for the resources requested by the pod we are trying to schedule.\\n305: func (f *Fit) isResourceScaleDown(targetPod, originalOtherPod, modifiedOtherPod *v1.Pod) bool {\\n306: \\tif modifiedOtherPod.Spec.NodeName == \\\"\\\" {\\n307: \\t\\t// no resource is freed up whatever the pod is modified.\\n308: \\t\\treturn false\\n309: \\t}\\n310: \\n311: \\t// the other pod was scheduled, so modification or deletion may free up some resources.\\n312: \\t\\tdefault:\\n337: \\t\\t\\tif schedutil.IsScalarResourceName(rName) && originalMaxResourceReq.ScalarResources[rName] > modifiedMaxResourceReq.ScalarResources[rName] {\\n338: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn \\n374: // Filter invoked at the filter extension point.\\n375: // Checks if a node has sufficient resources, such as cpu, memory, gpu, opaque int resources etc to run a pod.\\n376: // It returns a list of insufficient resources, if empty, then the node has all the resources requested by the pod.\\n377: func (f *Fit) Filter(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, *config.NodeResourcesFitArgs) *resourceAllocationScorer {\\n68: \\t\\tresources := args.ScoringStrategy.Resources\\n69: \\t\\treturn &resourceAllocationScorer{\\n70: \\t\\t\\tName: We don't really copy the data since\\n118: // there is no need for that.\\n119: func (s *preScoreState) Clone() framework.StateData {\\n120: \\treturn s\\n121: }\\n122: \\n123: // PreScore calculates incoming pod's resource requests and writes them to the cycle state used.\\n124: func (f *Fit) PreScore(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodes []*framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\\n125: \\tstate := &preScoreState{\\n126: \\t\\tpodRequests: f.calculatePodResourceRequestList(pod, f.resources),\\n127: \\t}\\n128: \\tcycleState.Write(preScoreStateKey, state)\\n129: \\treturn nil\\n130: }\\n131: \\n132: func getPreScoreState(cycleState *framework.CycleState) (*preScoreState, error) {\\n133: \\tc, err := cycleState.Read(preScoreStateKey)\\n134: \\tif err != nil {\\n135: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"reading %q from cycleState: %w\\\", preScoreStateKey, err)\\n136: \\t}\\n137: \\n138: \\ts, ok := c.(*preScoreState)\\n139: \\tif !ok {\\n140: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid PreScore state, got type %T\\\", c)\\n141: \\t}\\n142: \\treturn s, nil\\n143: }\\n144: \\n145: // Name returns name of the plugin. It is used in logs, etc.\\n146: func (f *Fit) Name() string {\\n147: \\treturn Name\\n148: }\\n149: \\n150: // NewFit initializes a new plugin and returns it.\\n151: func NewFit(_ context.Context, plArgs runtime.Object, h framework.Handle, fts feature.Features) (framework.Plugin, error) {\\n152: \\targs, ok := plArgs.(*config.NodeResourcesFitArgs)\\n153: \\tif !ok {\\n154: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"want args to be of type NodeResourcesFitArgs, got %T\\\", plArgs)\\n155: \\t}\\n156: \\tif err := validation.ValidateNodeResourcesFitArgs(nil, args); err != nil {\\n157: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n158: \\t}\\n159: \\n160: \\tif args.ScoringStrategy == nil {\\n161: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"scoring strategy not specified\\\")\\n162: \\t}\\n163: \\n164: \\tstrategy := args.ScoringStrategy.Type\\n165: \\tscorePlugin, exists := nodeResourceStrategyTypeMap[strategy]\\n166: \\tif !exists {\\n167: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"scoring strategy %s is not supported\\\", strategy)\\n168: \\t}\\n169: \\n170: \\treturn &Fit{\\n171: \\t\\tignoredResources:                sets.New(args.IgnoredResources...),\\n172: \\t\\tignoredResourceGroups:           sets.New(args.IgnoredResourceGroups...),\\n173: \\t\\tenableInPlacePodVerticalScaling: fts.EnableInPlacePodVerticalScaling,\\n174: \\t\\tenableSidecarContainers:         fts.EnableSidecarContainers,\\n175: \\t\\thandle:                          h,\\n176: \\t\\tresourceAllocationScorer:        *scorePlugin(args),\\n177: \\t}, nil\\n178: }\\n179: \\n180: // computePodResourceRequest returns a framework.Resource that covers the largest\\n181: // width in each resource dimension. Because init-containers run sequentially, we collect\\n182: // the max in each dimension iteratively. In contrast, we sum the resource vectors for\\n183: // regular containers since they run simultaneously.\\n184: //\\n185: // # The resources defined for Overhead should be added to the calculated Resource request sum\\n186: //\\n187: // Example:\\n188: //\\n189: // Pod:\\n190: //\\n191: //\\tInitContainers\\n192: //\\t  IC1:\\n193: //\\t    CPU: 2\\n194: //\\t    Memory: 1G\\n195: //\\t  IC2:\\n196: //\\t    CPU: 2\\n197: //\\t    Memory: 3G\\n198: //\\tContainers\\n199: //\\t  C1:\\n200: //\\t    CPU: 2\\n201: //\\t    Memory: 1G\\n202: //\\t  C2:\\n203: //\\t    CPU: 1\\n204: //\\t    Memory: 1G\\n205: //\\n206: // Result: CPU: 3, Memory: 3G\\n207: func computePodResourceRequest(pod *v1.Pod) *preFilterState {\\n208: \\t// pod hasn't scheduled yet so we don't need to worry about InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled\\n209: \\treqs := resource.PodRequests(pod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{})\\n210: \\tresult := &preFilterState{}\\n211: \\tresult.SetMaxResource(reqs)\\n212: \\treturn result\\n213: }\\n214: \\n215: // PreFilter invoked at the prefilter extension point.\\n216: func (f *Fit) PreFilter(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (*framework.PreFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\\n217: \\tif !f.enableSidecarContainers && hasRestartableInitContainer(pod) {\\n218: \\t\\t// Scheduler will calculate resources usage for a Pod containing\\n219: \\t\\t// restartable init containers that will be equal or more than kubelet will\\n220: \\t\\t// require to run the Pod. So there will be no overbooking. However, to\\n221: \\t\\t// avoid the inconsistency in resource calculation between the scheduler\\n222: \\t\\t// and the older (before v1.28) kubelet, make the Pod unschedulable.\\n223: \\t\\treturn nil, framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, \\\"Pod has a restartable init container and the SidecarContainers feature is disabled\\\")\\n224: \\t}\\n225: \\tcycleState.Write(preFilterStateKey, computePodResourceRequest(pod))\\n226: \\treturn nil, nil\\n227: }\\n228: \\n229: // PreFilterExtensions returns prefilter extensions, pod add and remove.\\n230: func (f *Fit) PreFilterExtensions() framework.PreFilterExtensions {\\n231: \\treturn nil\\n232: }\\n233: \\n234: func getPreFilterState(cycleState *framework.CycleState) (*preFilterState, error) {\\n235: \\tc, err := cycleState.Read(preFilterStateKey)\\n236: \\tif err != nil {\\n237: \\t\\t// preFilterState doesn't exist, likely PreFilter wasn't invoked.\\n238: \\t\\treturn nil, Pod\\n249: // failed by this plugin schedulable.\\n250: func (f *Fit) EventsToRegister() []framework.ClusterEventWithHint {\\n251: \\tpodActionType := framework.Delete\\n252: \\tif f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling {\\n253: \\t\\t// If InPlacePodVerticalScaling (KEP 1287) is enabled, then PodUpdate event should be registered\\n254: \\t\\t// for this plugin since a Pod update may ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}, QueueingHintFn: f.isSchedulableAfterNodeChange},\\n260: \\t}\\n261: }\\n262: \\n263: // isSchedulableAfterPodChange is invoked whenever a pod deleted or updated. It checks whether\\n264: // that change made a previously unschedulable pod schedulable.\\n265: func (f *Fit) isSchedulableAfterPodChange(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, oldObj, newObj interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\\n266: \\toriginalPod, modifiedPod, err := schedutil.As[*v1.Pod](oldObj, newObj)\\n267: \\tif err != nil {\\n268: \\t\\treturn framework.Queue, err\\n269: \\t}\\n270: \\n271: \\tif modifiedPod == nil {\\n272: \\t\\tif originalPod.Spec.NodeName == \\\"\\\" {\\n273: \\t\\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"the deleted pod was unscheduled and it wouldn't make the unscheduled pod schedulable\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"deletedPod\\\", klog.KObj(originalPod))\\n274: \\t\\t\\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\\n275: \\t\\t}\\n276: \\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"another scheduled pod was deleted, and it may make the unscheduled pod schedulable\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"deletedPod\\\", klog.KObj(originalPod))\\n277: \\t\\treturn framework.Queue, nil\\n278: \\t}\\n279: \\n280: \\tif !f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling {\\n281: \\t\\t// If InPlacePodVerticalScaling (KEP 1287) is disabled, it cannot free up resources.\\n282: \\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"another pod was modified, but InPlacePodVerticalScaling is disabled, so it doesn't make the unscheduled pod schedulable\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"modifiedPod\\\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod))\\n283: \\t\\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\\n284: \\t}\\n285: \\n286: \\t// Modifications may or may not be relevant. We only care about modifications that\\n287: \\t// change the other pod's resource request and the resource is also requested by the\\n288: \\t// pod we are trying to schedule.\\n289: \\tif !f.isResourceScaleDown(pod, originalPod, modifiedPod) {\\n290: \\t\\tif loggerV := logger.V(10); loggerV.Enabled() {\\n291: \\t\\t\\t// Log more information.\\n292: \\t\\t\\tloggerV.Info(\\\"another Pod got modified, but the modification isn't related to the resource request\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"modifiedPod\\\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod), \\\"diff\\\", cmp.Diff(originalPod, modifiedPod))\\n293: \\t\\t} else {\\n294: \\t\\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"another Pod got modified, but the modification isn't related to the resource request\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), }\\n302: \\n303: // isResourceScaleDown checks whether the resource request of the modified pod is less than the original pod\\n304: // for the resources requested by the pod we are trying to schedule.\\n305: func (f *Fit) isResourceScaleDown(targetPod, originalOtherPod, modifiedOtherPod *v1.Pod) bool {\\n306: \\tif modifiedOtherPod.Spec.NodeName == \\\"\\\" {\\n307: \\t\\t// no resource is freed up whatever the pod is modified.\\n308: \\t\\treturn false\\n309: \\t}\\n310: \\n311: \\t// the other pod was scheduled, so modification or deletion may free up some resources.\\n312: \\toriginalMaxResourceReq, modifiedMaxResourceReq := &framework.Resource{}, &framework.Resource{}\\n313: \\toriginalMaxResourceReq.SetMaxResource(resource.PodRequests(originalOtherPod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled: f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling}))\\n314: \\tmodifiedMaxResourceReq.SetMaxResource(resource.PodRequests(modifiedOtherPod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled: f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling}))\\n315: \\n316: \\t// check whether the resource request of the modified pod is less than the original pod.\\n317: \\tpodRequests := resource.PodRequests(targetPod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled: f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling})\\n318: \\tfor rName, rValue := \\t\\tswitch rName {\\n324: \\t\\tcase v1.ResourceCPU:\\n325: \\t\\t\\tif originalMaxResourceReq.MilliCPU > modifiedMaxResourceReq.MilliCPU {\\n326: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n327: \\t\\t\\t}\\n328: \\t\\tcase v1.ResourceMemory:\\n329: \\t\\t\\tif originalMaxResourceReq.Memory > modifiedMaxResourceReq.Memory {\\n330: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n331: \\t\\t\\t}\\n332: \\t\\tcase v1.ResourceEphemeralStorage:\\n333: \\t\\t\\tif originalMaxResourceReq.EphemeralStorage > modifiedMaxResourceReq.EphemeralStorage {\\n334: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n335: \\t\\t\\t}\\n336: \\t\\tdefault:\\n337: \\t\\t\\tif schedutil.IsScalarResourceName(rName) && originalMaxResourceReq.ScalarResources[rName] > modifiedMaxResourceReq.ScalarResources[rName] {\\n338: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n339: \\t\\t\\t}\\n340: \\t\\t}\\n341: \\t}\\n342: \\treturn false\\n343: }\\n344: \\n345: // isSchedulableAfterNodeChange is invoked whenever a node added or changed. It checks whether\\n346: // that change made a previously unschedulable pod schedulable.\\n347: func (f *Fit) isSchedulableAfterNodeChange(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, oldObj, newObj interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\\n348: \\t_, modifiedNode, err := schedutil.As[*v1.Node](oldObj, newObj)\\n349: \\tif err != nil {\\n350: \\t\\treturn framework.Queue, err\\n351: \\t}\\n352: \\t// TODO: also check if the original node meets the pod's resource requestments once preCheck is completely removed.\\n353: \\t// See: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/110175\\n354: \\tif isFit(pod, modifiedNode) {\\n355: \\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"node was updated, and may fit with the pod's resource requestments\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"node\\\", klog.KObj(modifiedNode))\\n356: \\t\\treturn framework.Queue, nil\\n357: \\t}\\n358: \\n359: \\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"node was created or updated, but it doesn't have enough resource(s) to accommodate this pod\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"node\\\", klog.KObj(modifiedNode))\\n360: \\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\\n361: }\\n362: \\n363: // isFit checks if the pod fits the node. \\n374: // Filter invoked at the filter extension point.\\n375: // Checks if a node has sufficient resources, such as cpu, memory, gpu, opaque int resources etc to run a pod.\\n376: // It returns a list of insufficient resources, if empty, then the node has all the resources requested by the pod.\\n377: func (f *Fit) Filter(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, \\t\\t\\t})\\n497: \\t\\t}\\n498: \\t}\\n499: \\n500: \\treturn insufficientResources\\n501: }\\n502: \\n503: // Score invoked at the Score extension point.\\n504: func (f *Fit) Score(ctx Because init-containers run sequentially, we collect\n182: // the max in each dimension iteratively. In contrast, we sum the resource vectors for\n183: // regular containers since they run simultaneously.\n184: //\n185: // # The resources defined for Overhead should be added to the calculated Resource request sum\n186: //\n187: // Example:\n188: //\n189: // Pod:\n190: //\n191: //\tInitContainers\n192: //\t  IC1:\n193: //\t    CPU: 2\n194: //\t    Memory: 1G\n195: //\t  IC2:\n196: //\t    CPU: 2\n197: //\t    Memory: 3G\n198: //\tContainers\n199: //\t  C1:\n200: //\t    CPU: 2\n201: //\t    Memory: 1G\n202: //\t  C2:\n203: //\t    CPU: 1\n204: //\t    Memory: 1G\n205: //\n206: // Result: CPU: 3, Memory: 3G\n207: func computePodResourceRequest(pod *v1.Pod) *preFilterState {\n208: \t// pod hasn't scheduled yet so we don't need to worry about InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled\n209: \treqs := resource.PodRequests(pod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{})\n210: \tresult := &preFilterState{}\n211: \tresult.SetMaxResource(reqs)\n212: \treturn result\n213: }\n214: \n215: // PreFilter invoked at the prefilter extension point.\n216: func (f *Fit) PreFilter(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (*framework.PreFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\n217: \tif !f.enableSidecarContainers && hasRestartableInitContainer(pod) {\n218: \t\t// Scheduler will calculate resources usage for a Pod containing\n219: \t\t// restartable init containers that will be equal or more than kubelet will\n220: \t\t// require to run the Pod. So there will be no overbooking. However, to\n221: \t\t// avoid the inconsistency in resource calculation between the scheduler\n222: \t\t// and the older (before v1.28) kubelet, make the Pod unschedulable.\n223: \t\treturn nil, framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, \"Pod has a restartable init container and the SidecarContainers feature is disabled\")\n224: \t}\n225: \tcycleState.Write(preFilterStateKey, computePodResourceRequest(pod))\n226: \treturn nil, nil\n227: }\n228: \n229: // PreFilterExtensions returns prefilter extensions, pod add and remove.\n230: func (f *Fit) PreFilterExtensions() framework.PreFilterExtensions {\n231: \treturn nil\n232: }\n233: \n234: func getPreFilterState(cycleState *framework.CycleState) (*preFilterState, error) {\n235: \tc, error\", c)\n244: \t}\n245: \treturn s, nil\n246: }\n247: \n248: // EventsToRegister returns the possible events that may make a Pod\n249: // failed by this plugin schedulable.\n250: func (f *Fit) EventsToRegister() []framework.ClusterEventWithHint {\n251: \tpodActionType := framework.Delete\n252: \tif f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling {\n253: \t\t// If InPlacePodVerticalScaling (KEP 1287) is enabled, then PodUpdate event should be registered\n254: \t\t// for this plugin since a Pod update may free up resources that make other Pods schedulable.\n255: \t\tpodActionType |= framework.Update\n256: \t}\n257: \treturn []framework.ClusterEventWithHint{\n258: \t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Pod, ActionType: podActionType}, QueueingHintFn: f.isSchedulableAfterPodChange},\n259: \t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Node, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}, QueueingHintFn: f.isSchedulableAfterNodeChange},\n260: \t}\n261: }\n262: \n263: // isSchedulableAfterPodChange is invoked whenever a pod deleted or updated. It checks whether\n264: // that change made a previously unschedulable pod schedulable.\n265: func (f *Fit) isSchedulableAfterPodChange(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, oldObj, newObj interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\n266: \toriginalPod, modifiedPod, err := schedutil.As[*v1.Pod](oldObj, newObj)\n267: \tif err != nil {\n268: \t\treturn framework.Queue, err\n269: \t}\n270: \n271: \tif modifiedPod == nil {\n272: \t\tif originalPod.Spec.NodeName == \"\" {\n273: \t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"the deleted pod was unscheduled and it wouldn't make the unscheduled pod schedulable\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"deletedPod\", klog.KObj(originalPod))\n274: \t\t\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\n275: \t\t}\n276: \t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"another scheduled pod was deleted, and it may make the unscheduled pod schedulable\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"deletedPod\", klog.KObj(originalPod))\n277: \t\treturn framework.Queue, nil\n278: \t}\n279: \n280: \tif !f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling {\n281: \t\t// If InPlacePodVerticalScaling (KEP 1287) is disabled, it cannot free up resources.\n282: \t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"another pod was modified, but InPlacePodVerticalScaling is disabled, so it doesn't make the unscheduled pod schedulable\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"modifiedPod\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod))\n283: \t\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\n284: \t}\n285: \n286: \t// Modifications may or may not be relevant. We only care about modifications that\n287: \t// change the other pod's resource request and the resource is also requested by the\n288: \t// pod we are trying to schedule.\n289: \tif !f.isResourceScaleDown(pod, originalPod, modifiedPod) {\n290: \t\tif loggerV := logger.V(10); loggerV.Enabled() {\n291: \t\t\t// Log more information.\n292: \t\t\tloggerV.Info(\"another Pod got modified, but the modification isn't related to the resource request\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"modifiedPod\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod), \"diff\", cmp.Diff(originalPod, modifiedPod))\n293: \t\t} else {\n294: \t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"another Pod got modified, but the modification isn't related to the resource request\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"modifiedPod\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod))\n295: \t\t}\n296: \t\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\n297: \t}\n298: \n299: \tlogger.V(5).Info(\"the max request resources of another scheduled pod got reduced and it may make the unscheduled pod schedulable\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"modifiedPod\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod))\n300: \treturn framework.Queue, nil\n301: }\n302: \n303: // isResourceScaleDown checks whether the resource request of the modified pod is less than the original pod\n304: // for the resources requested by the pod we are trying to schedule.\n305: func (f *Fit) isResourceScaleDown(targetPod, originalOtherPod, modifiedOtherPod *v1.Pod) bool {\n306: \tif modifiedOtherPod.Spec.NodeName == \"\" {\n307: \t\t// no resource is freed up whatever the pod is modified.\n308: \t\treturn false\n309: \t}\n310: \n311: \t// the other pod was scheduled, so modification or deletion may free up some resources.\n312: \toriginalMaxResourceReq, modifiedMaxResourceReq := &framework.Resource{}, &framework.Resource{}\n313: \toriginalMaxResourceReq.SetMaxResource(resource.PodRequests(originalOtherPod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled: f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling}))\n314: \tmodifiedMaxResourceReq.SetMaxResource(resource.PodRequests(modifiedOtherPod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled: f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling}))\n315: \n316: \t// check whether the resource request of the modified pod is less than the original pod.\n317: \tpodRequests := resource.PodRequests(targetPod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled: f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling})\n318: \tfor rName, rValue := range podRequests {\n319: \t\tif rValue.IsZero() {\n320: \t\t\t// We only care about the resources requested by the pod we are trying to schedule.\n321: \t\t\tcontinue\n322: \t\t}\n323: \t\tswitch rName {\n324: \t\tcase v1.ResourceCPU:\n325: \t\t\tif originalMaxResourceReq.MilliCPU > modifiedMaxResourceReq.MilliCPU {\n326: \t\t\t\treturn true\n327: \t\t\t}\n328: \t\tcase v1.ResourceMemory:\n329: \t\t\tif originalMaxResourceReq.Memory > modifiedMaxResourceReq.Memory {\n330: \t\t\t\treturn true\n331: \t\t\t}\n332: \t\tcase v1.ResourceEphemeralStorage:\n333: \t\t\tif originalMaxResourceReq.EphemeralStorage > modifiedMaxResourceReq.EphemeralStorage {\n334: \t\t\t\treturn true\n335: \t\t\t}\n336: \t\tdefault:\n337: \t\t\tif schedutil.IsScalarResourceName(rName) && originalMaxResourceReq.ScalarResources[rName] > modifiedMaxResourceReq.ScalarResources[rName] {\n338: \t\t\t\treturn true\n339: \t\t\t}\n340: \t\t}\n341: \t}\n342: \treturn false\n343: }\n344: \n345: // isSchedulableAfterNodeChange is invoked whenever a node added or changed. It checks whether\n346: // that change made a previously unschedulable pod schedulable.\n347: func (f *Fit) isSchedulableAfterNodeChange(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, oldObj, newObj interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\n348: \t_, modifiedNode, err := schedutil.As[*v1.Node](oldObj, newObj)\n349: \tif err != nil {\n350: \t\treturn framework.Queue, err\n351: \t}\n352: \t// TODO: also check if the original node meets the pod's resource requestments once preCheck is completely removed.\n353: \t// See: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/110175\n354: \tif isFit(pod, modifiedNode) {\n355: \t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"node was updated, and may fit with the pod's resource requestments\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(modifiedNode))\n356: \t\treturn framework.Queue, nil\n357: \t}\n358: \n359: \tlogger.V(5).Info(\"node was created or updated, but it doesn't have enough resource(s) to accommodate this pod\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(modifiedNode))\n360: \treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\n361: }\n362: \n363: // isFit checks if the pod fits the node. If the node is nil, it returns false.\n364: // It constructs a fake NodeInfo object for the node and checks if the pod fits the node.\n365: func isFit(pod *v1.Pod, node *v1.Node) bool {\n366: \tif node == nil {\n367: \t\treturn false\n368: \t}\n369: \tnodeInfo := framework.NewNodeInfo()\n370: \tnodeInfo.SetNode(node)\n371: \treturn len(Fits(pod, nodeInfo)) == 0\n372: }\n373: \n374: // Filter invoked at the filter extension point.\n375: // Checks if a node has sufficient resources, such as cpu, memory, gpu, opaque int resources etc to run a pod.\n376: // It returns a list of insufficient resources, if empty, then the node has all the resources requested by the pod.\n377: func (f *Fit) Filter(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n378: \ts, err := getPreFilterState(cycleState)\n379: \tif err != nil {\n380: \t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n381: \t}\n382: \n383: \tinsufficientResources := fitsRequest(s, nodeInfo, f.ignoredResources, f.ignoredResourceGroups)\n384: \n385: \tif len(insufficientResources) != 0 {\n386: \t\t// We will keep all failure reasons.\n387: \t\tfailureReasons := make([]string, 0, len(insufficientResources))\n388: \t\tfor i := range insufficientResources {\n389: \t\t\tfailureReasons = append(failureReasons, insufficientResources[i].Reason)\n390: \t\t}\n391: \t\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Unschedulable, failureReasons...)\n392: \t}\n393: \treturn nil\n394: }\n395: \n396: func hasRestartableInitContainer(pod *v1.Pod) bool {\n397: \tfor _, c := range pod.Spec.InitContainers {\n398: \t\tif c.RestartPolicy != nil && *c.RestartPolicy == v1.ContainerRestartPolicyAlways {\n399: \t\t\treturn true\n400: \t\t}\n401: \t}\n402: \treturn false\n403: }\n404: \n405: // InsufficientResource describes what kind of resource limit is hit and caused the pod to not fit the node.\n406: type InsufficientResource struct {\n407: \tResourceName v1.ResourceName\n408: \t// We explicitly have a parameter for reason to avoid formatting a message on the fly\n409: \t// for common resources, which is expensive for cluster autoscaler simulations.\n410: \tReason    string\n411: \tRequested int64\n412: \tUsed      int64\n413: \tCapacity  int64\n414: }\n415: \n416: // Fits checks if node have enough resources to host the pod.\n417: func Fits(pod *v1.Pod, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) []InsufficientResource {\n418: \treturn fitsRequest(computePodResourceRequest(pod), nodeInfo, nil, nil)\n419: }\n420: \n421: func fitsRequest(podRequest *preFilterState, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo, ignoredExtendedResources, ignoredResourceGroups sets.Set[string]) []InsufficientResource {\n422: \tinsufficientResources := make([]InsufficientResource, 0, 4)\n423: \n424: \tallowedPodNumber := nodeInfo.Allocatable.AllowedPodNumber\n425: \tif len(nodeInfo.Pods)+1 > allowedPodNumber {\n426: \t\tinsufficientResources = append(insufficientResources, InsufficientResource{\n427: \t\t\tResourceName: v1.ResourcePods,\n428: \t\t\tReason:       \"Too many pods\",\n429: \t\t\tRequested:    1,\n430: \t\t\tUsed:         int64(len(nodeInfo.Pods)),\n431: \t\t\tCapacity:     int64(allowedPodNumber),\n432: \t\t})\n433: \t}\n434: \n435: \tif podRequest.MilliCPU == 0 &&\n436: \t\tpodRequest.Memory == 0 &&\n437: \t\tpodRequest.EphemeralStorage == 0 &&\n438: \t\tlen(podRequest.ScalarResources) == 0 {\n439: \t\treturn insufficientResources\n440: \t}\n441: \n442: \tif podRequest.MilliCPU > 0 && podRequest.MilliCPU > (nodeInfo.Allocatable.MilliCPU-nodeInfo.Requested.MilliCPU) {\n443: \t\tinsufficientResources = append(insufficientResources, InsufficientResource{\n444: \t\t\tResourceName: v1.ResourceCPU,\n445: \t\t\tReason:       \"Insufficient cpu\",\n446: \t\t\tRequested:    podRequest.MilliCPU,\n447: \t\t\tUsed:         nodeInfo.Requested.MilliCPU,\n448: \t\t\tCapacity:     nodeInfo.Allocatable.MilliCPU,\n449: \t\t})\n450: \t}\n451: \tif podRequest.Memory > 0 && podRequest.Memory > (nodeInfo.Allocatable.Memory-nodeInfo.Requested.Memory) {\n452: \t\tinsufficientResources = append(insufficientResources, InsufficientResource{\n453: \t\t\tResourceName: v1.ResourceMemory,\n454: \t\t\tReason:       \"Insufficient memory\",\n455: \t\t\tRequested:    podRequest.Memory,\n456: \t\t\tUsed:         nodeInfo.Requested.Memory,\n457: \t\t\tCapacity:     nodeInfo.Allocatable.Memory,\n458: \t\t})\n459: \t}\n460: \tif podRequest.EphemeralStorage > 0 &&\n461: \t\tpodRequest.EphemeralStorage > (nodeInfo.Allocatable.EphemeralStorage-nodeInfo.Requested.EphemeralStorage) {\n462: \t\tinsufficientResources = append(insufficientResources, InsufficientResource{\n463: \t\t\tResourceName: v1.ResourceEphemeralStorage,\n464: \t\t\tReason:       \"Insufficient ephemeral-storage\",\n465: \t\t\tRequested:    podRequest.EphemeralStorage,\n466: \t\t\tUsed:         nodeInfo.Requested.EphemeralStorage,\n467: \t\t\tCapacity:     nodeInfo.Allocatable.EphemeralStorage,\n468: \t\t})\n469: \t}\n470: \n471: \tfor rName, rQuant := range podRequest.ScalarResources {\n472: \t\t// Skip in case request quantity is zero\n473: \t\tif rQuant == 0 {\n474: \t\t\tcontinue\n475: \t\t}\n476: \n477: \t\tif v1helper.IsExtendedResourceName(rName) {\n478: \t\t\t// If this resource is one of the extended resources that should be ignored, we will skip checking it.\n479: \t\t\t// rName is guaranteed to have a slash due to API validation.\n480: \t\t\tvar rNamePrefix string\n481: \t\t\tif ignoredResourceGroups.Len() > 0 {\n482: \t\t\t\trNamePrefix = strings.Split(string(rName), \"/\")[0]\n483: \t\t\t}\n484: \t\t\tif ignoredExtendedResources.Has(string(rName)) || ignoredResourceGroups.Has(rNamePrefix) {\n485: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n486: \t\t\t}\n487: \t\t}\n488: \n489: \t\tif rQuant > (nodeInfo.Allocatable.ScalarResources[rName] - nodeInfo.Requested.ScalarResources[rName]) {\n490: \t\t\tinsufficientResources = append(insufficientResources, InsufficientResource{\n491: \t\t\t\tResourceName: rName,\n492: \t\t\t\tReason:       fmt.Sprintf(\"Insufficient %v\", rName),\n493: \t\t\t\tRequested:    podRequest.ScalarResources[rName],\n494: \t\t\t\tUsed:         nodeInfo.Requested.ScalarResources[rName],\n495: \t\t\t\tCapacity:     nodeInfo.Allocatable.ScalarResources[rName],\n496: \t\t\t})\n497: \t\t}\n498: \t}\n499: \n500: \treturn insufficientResources\n501: }\n502: \n503: // Score invoked at the Score extension point.\n504: func (f *Fit) Score(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *framework.Status) {\n505: \tnodeInfo, err := f.handle.SnapshotSharedLister().NodeInfos().Get(nodeName)\n506: \tif err != nil {\n507: \t\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\"getting node root square of Σ((fraction(i)-mean)^2)/len(fractions)\n157: \t// Otherwise, set the std to zero is enough.\n158: \tif len(resourceToFractions) == 2 {\n159: \t\tstd = math.Abs((resourceToFractions[0] - resourceToFractions[1]) / 2)\n160: \n161: \t} else if len(resourceToFractions) > 2 {\n162: \t\tmean := totalFraction / float64(len(resourceToFractions))\n163: \t\tvar sum float64\n164: \t\tfor _, fraction := range resourceToFractions {\n165: \t\t\tsum = sum + (fraction-mean)*(fraction-mean)\n166: \t\t}\n167: \t\tstd = math.Sqrt(sum / float64(len(resourceToFractions)))\n168: \t}\n169: \n170: \t// STD (standard deviation) is always a positive value. 1-deviation lets the score to be higher for node which has least deviation and\n171: \t// multiplying it with `MaxNodeScore` provides the scaling factor needed.\n172: \treturn int64((1 - std) * float64(framework.MaxNodeScore))\n173: }\n174: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/noderesources/balanced_allocation.go"}}}

\\t\\\"math\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"sync/atomic\\\"\\n24: \\n25: \\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n26: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n28: \\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework\\\"\\n29: )\\n30: \\n31: // preScoreStateKey is the key in CycleState to InterPodAffinity pre-computed data for Scoring.\\n32: const preScoreStateKey = \\\"PreScore\\\" + Name\\n33: \\n34: type scoreMap map[string]map[string]int64\\n35: \\n36: // preScoreState computed at PreScore and used at Score.\\n37: type preScoreState struct {\\n38: \\ttopologyScore scoreMap\\n39: \\tpodInfo       *framework.PodInfo\\n40: \\t// A copy of the incoming pod's namespace labels.\\n41: \\tnamespaceLabels labels.Set\\n42: }\\n43: \\n44: // Clone implements the mandatory Clone interface. We don't really copy the data since\\n45: // there is no need for that.\\n46: func (s *preScoreState) Clone() framework.StateData {\\n47: \\treturn s\\n48: }\\n49: \\n50: func (m scoreMap) processTerm(term *framework.AffinityTerm, weight int32, pod *v1.Pod, nsLabels labels.Set, node *v1.Node, multiplier int32) {\\n51: \\tif term.Matches(pod, nsLabels) {\\n52: \\t\\tif tpValue, \\t\\t\\tm[term.TopologyKey][tpValue] += int64(weight * multiplier)\\n57: \\t\\t}\\n58: \\t}\\n59: }\\n60: \\n61: func (m scoreMap) processTerms(terms []framework.WeightedAffinityTerm, pod *v1.Pod, \\t}\\n65: }\\n66: \\n67: func (m scoreMap) append(other scoreMap) {\\n68: \\tfor topology, oScores := range other {\\n69: \\t\\tscores := m[topology]\\n70: \\t\\tif scores == nil {\\n71: \\t\\t\\tm[topology] = oScores\\n72: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n73: \\t\\t}\\n74: \\t\\tfor k, v := range oScores {\\n75: \\t\\t\\tscores[k] += v\\n76: \\t\\t}\\n77: \\t}\\n78: }\\n79: \\n80: func (pl \\ttopoScore scoreMap,\\n86: ) {\\n87: \\texistingPodNode := existingPodNodeInfo.Node()\\n88: \\tif len(existingPodNode.Labels) == 0 {\\n89: \\t\\treturn\\n90: \\t}\\n91: \\n92: \\t// For every soft pod affinity term of <pod>, if <existingPod> matches the term,\\n93: \\t// increment <p.counts> for every node in the cluster with the same <term.TopologyKey>\\n94: \\t// value as that of <existingPods>`s node by the term`s weight.\\n95: \\t// Note that the incoming pod's terms have the namespaceSelector merged into the namespaces, and so\\n96: \\t// here we don't lookup the existing pod's namespace labels, hence passing nil for nsLabels.\\n97: \\ttopoScore.processTerms(state.podInfo.PreferredAffinityTerms, existingPod.Pod, nil, existingPodNode, 1)\\n98: \\n99: \\t// For every soft pod anti-affinity term of <pod>, if <existingPod> matches the term,\\n100: \\t// decrement <p.counts> for every node in the cluster with the same <term.TopologyKey>\\n101: \\t// value as that of <existingPod>`s node by the term`s weight.\\n102: \\t// Note that the incoming pod's terms have the namespaceSelector merged into the namespaces, and so\\n103: \\t// here we don't lookup the existing pod's namespace labels, hence passing nil for nsLabels.\\n104: \\ttopoScore.processTerms(state.podInfo.PreferredAntiAffinityTerms, existingPod.Pod, nil, existingPodNode, -1)\\n105: \\n106: \\t// For every hard pod affinity term of <existingPod>, if <pod> matches the term,\\n107: \\t// increment <p.counts> for every node in the cluster with the same <term.TopologyKey>\\n108: \\t// value as that of <existingPod>'s node by the constant <args.hardPodAffinityWeight>\\n109: \\tif pl.args.HardPodAffinityWeight > 0 && len(existingPodNode.Labels) != 0 {\\n110: \\t\\tfor _, t := range existingPod.RequiredAffinityTerms {\\n111: \\t\\t\\ttopoScore.processTerm(&t, pl.args.HardPodAffinityWeight, incomingPod, state.namespaceLabels, existingPodNode, 1)\\n112: \\t\\t}\\n113: \\t}\\n114: \\n115: \\t// For every soft pod affinity term of <existingPod>, if <pod> matches the term,\\n116: \\t// increment <p.counts> for every node in the cluster with the same <term.TopologyKey>\\n117: \\t// value as that of <existingPod>'s node by the term's weight.\\n118: \\ttopoScore.processTerms(existingPod.PreferredAffinityTerms, incomingPod, state.namespaceLabels, existingPodNode, 1)\\n119: \\n120: \\t// For every soft pod anti-affinity term of <existingPod>, if <pod> matches the term,\\n121: \\t// decrement <pm.counts> for every node in the cluster with the same <term.TopologyKey>\\n122: \\t// value as that of <existingPod>'s node by the term's weight.\\n123: \\ttopoScore.processTerms(existingPod.PreferredAntiAffinityTerms, incomingPod, state.namespaceLabels, existingPodNode, -1)\\n124: }\\n125: \\n126: // PreScore builds and writes cycle state used by Score and NormalizeScore.\\n127: func (pl *InterPodAffinity) PreScore(\\n128: \\tpCtx context.Context,\\n129: \\tcycleState *framework.CycleState,\\n130: \\tpod *v1.Pod,\\n131: \\tnodes []*framework.NodeInfo,\\n132: ) *framework.Status {\\n133: \\tif len(nodes) == 0 {\\n134: \\t\\t// No nodes to score.\\n135: \\t\\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Skip)\\n136: \\t}\\n137: \\n138: \\tif pl.sharedLister == nil {\\n139: \\t\\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Error, \\\"empty shared lister in InterPodAffinity PreScore\\\")\\n140: \\t}\\n141: \\n142: \\taffinity := pod.Spec.Affinity\\n143: \\thasPreferredAffinityConstraints := affinity != nil && affinity.PodAffinity != nil && len(affinity.PodAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution) > 0\\n144: \\thasPreferredAntiAffinityConstraints := affinity != nil && affinity.PodAntiAffinity != nil && len(affinity.PodAntiAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution) > 0\\n145: \\thasConstraints := hasPreferredAffinityConstraints || hasPreferredAntiAffinityConstraints\\n146: \\n147: \\t// Optionally ignore calculating preferences of existing pods' affinity rules\\n148: \\t// if the incoming pod has no inter-pod affinities.\\n149: \\tif pl.args.IgnorePreferredTermsOfExistingPods && !hasConstraints {\\n150: \\t\\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Skip)\\n151: \\t}\\n152: \\n153: \\t// Unless the pod being scheduled has preferred affinity terms, we only\\n154: \\t// need to process nodes hosting pods with affinity.\\n155: \\tvar allNodes []*framework.NodeInfo\\n156: \\tvar err error\\n157: \\tif hasConstraints {\\n158: \\t\\tallNodes, err = pl.sharedLister.NodeInfos().List()\\n159: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n160: \\t\\t\\treturn framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to get all nodes from shared lister: %w\\\", err))\\n161: \\t\\t}\\n162: \\t} else {\\n163: \\t\\tallNodes, err = pl.sharedLister.NodeInfos().HavePodsWithAffinityList()\\n164: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n165: \\t\\t\\treturn framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to get pods with affinity list: %w\\\", err))\\n166: \\t\\t}\\n167: \\t}\\n168: \\n169: \\tstate := &preScoreState{\\n170: \\t\\ttopologyScore: make(map[string]map[string]int64),\\n171: \\t}\\n172: \\n173: \\tif state.podInfo, err = framework.NewPodInfo(pod); err != nil {\\n174: \\t\\t// Ideally we never reach here, because errors will be caught by PreFilter\\n175: \\t\\treturn framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to parse pod: %w\\\", err))\\n176: \\t}\\n177: \\n178: \\tfor i := range state.podInfo.PreferredAffinityTerms {\\n179: \\t\\tif err := pl.mergeAffinityTermNamespacesIfNotEmpty(&state.podInfo.PreferredAffinityTerms[i].AffinityTerm); err != nil {\\n180: \\t\\t\\treturn framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\\\"updating PreferredAffinityTerms: %w\\\", err))\\n181: \\t\\t}\\n182: \\t}\\n183: \\tfor i := range state.podInfo.PreferredAntiAffinityTerms {\\n184: \\t\\tif err := pl.mergeAffinityTermNamespacesIfNotEmpty(&state.podInfo.PreferredAntiAffinityTerms[i].AffinityTerm); err != nil {\\n185: \\t\\t\\treturn framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\\\"updating PreferredAntiAffinityTerms: %w\\\", err))\\n186: \\t\\t}\\n187: \\t}\\n188: \\tlogger := klog.FromContext(pCtx)\\n189: \\tstate.namespaceLabels = GetNamespaceLabelsSnapshot(logger, pod.Namespace, pl.nsLister)\\n190: \\n191: \\ttopoScores := make([]scoreMap, len(allNodes))\\n192: \\tindex := int32(-1)\\n193: \\tprocessNode := func(i int) {\\n194: \\t\\tnodeInfo := allNodes[i]\\n195: \\n196: \\t\\t// Unless the pod being scheduled has preferred affinity terms, we only\\n197: \\t\\t// need to process pods with affinity in the node.\\n198: \\t\\tpodsToProcess := nodeInfo.PodsWithAffinity\\n199: \\t\\tif hasConstraints {\\n200: \\t\\t\\t// We need to process all the pods.\\n201: \\t\\t\\tpodsToProcess = nodeInfo.Pods\\n202: \\t\\t}\\n203: \\n204: \\t\\ttopoScore := make(scoreMap)\\n205: \\t\\tfor _, existingPod := range podsToProcess {\\n206: \\t\\t\\tpl.processExistingPod(state, existingPod, nodeInfo, pod, topoScore)\\n207: \\t\\t}\\n208: \\t\\tif len(topoScore) > 0 {\\n209: \\t\\t\\ttopoScores[atomic.AddInt32(&index, 1)] = topoScore\\n210: \\t\\t}\\n211: \\t}\\n212: \\tpl.parallelizer.Until(pCtx, len(allNodes), processNode, pl.Name())\\n213: \\n214: \\tif index == -1 {\\n215: \\t\\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Skip)\\n216: \\t}\\n217: \\n218: \\tfor i := 0; i <= int(index); i++ {\\n219: \\t\\tstate.topologyScore.append(topoScores[i])\\n220: \\t}\\n221: \\n222: \\tcycleState.Write(preScoreStateKey, state)\\n223: \\treturn nil\\n224: }\\n225: \\n226: func getPreScoreState(cycleState *framework.CycleState) (*preScoreState, error) {\\n227: \\tc, err := cycleState.Read(preScoreStateKey)\\n228: \\tif err != nil {\\n229: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to read %q from cycleState: %w\\\", preScoreStateKey, err)\\n230: \\t}\\n231: \\n232: \\ts, ok := c.(*preScoreState)\\n233: \\tif !ok {\\n234: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"%+v  convert to interpodaffinity.preScoreState error\\\", c)\\n235: \\t}\\n236: \\treturn s, nil\\n237: }\\n238: \\n239: // Score invoked at the Score extension point.\\n240: // The \\\"score\\\" returned in this function is the sum of weights got from cycleState which have its topologyKey matching with the node's labels.\\n241: // it is normalized later.\\n242: // Note: the returned \\\"score\\\" is positive for pod-affinity, and negative for pod-antiaffinity.\\n243: func (pl *InterPodAffinity) Score(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *framework.Status) {\\n244: \\tnodeInfo, err := pl.sharedLister.NodeInfos().Get(nodeName)\\n245: \\tif err != nil {\\n246: \\t\\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to get node %q from Snapshot: %w\\\", nodeName, err))\\n247: \\t}\\n248: \\tnode := nodeInfo.Node()\\n249: \\n250: \\ts, err := getPreScoreState(cycleState)\\n251: \\tif err != nil {\\n252: \\t\\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(err)\\n253: \\t}\\n254: \\tvar score int64\\n255: \\tfor tpKey, tpValues := range s.topologyScore {\\n256: \\t\\tif v, exist := node.Labels[tpKey]; exist {\\n257: \\t\\t\\tscore += tpValues[v]\\n258: \\t\\t}\\n259: \\t}\\n260: \\n261: \\treturn score, nil\\n262: }\\n263: \\n264: // NormalizeScore normalizes the score for each filteredNode.\\n265: func (pl *InterPodAffinity) NormalizeScore(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, scores framework.NodeScoreList) *framework.Status {\\n266: \\ts, err := getPreScoreState(cycleState)\\n267: \\tif err != nil {\\n268: \\t\\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\\n269: \\t}\\n270: \\tif len(s.topologyScore) == 0 {\\n271: \\t\\treturn nil\\n272: \\t}\\n273: \\n274: \\tvar minCount int64 = math.MaxInt64\\n275: \\tvar maxCount int64 = math.MinInt64\\n276: \\tfor i := range scores {\\n277: \\t\\tscore := scores[i].Score\\n278: \\t\\tif score > maxCount {\\n279: \\t\\t\\tmaxCount = score\\n280: \\t\\t}\\n281: \\t\\tif score < minCount {\\n282: \\t\\t\\tminCount = score\\n283: \\t\\t}\\n284: \\t}\\n285: \\n286: \\tmaxMinDiff := maxCount - minCount\\n287: \\tfor i := range scores {\\n288: \\t\\tfScore := float64(0)\\n289: \\t\\tif maxMinDiff > 0 {\\n290: \\t\\t\\tfScore = float64(framework.MaxNodeScore) * (float64(scores[i].Score-minCount) / float64(maxMinDiff))\\n291: \\t\\t}\\n292: \\n293: \\t\\tscores[i].Score = int64(fScore)\\n294: \\t}\\n295: \\n296: \\treturn nil\\n297: }\\n298: \\n299: // ScoreExtensions of the Score plugin.\\n300: func (pl *InterPodAffinity) ScoreExtensions() framework.ScoreExtensions {\\n301: \\treturn pl\\n302: }\\n303:
